Author: Test User <test@example.com>
Date:   Thu Apr 30 13:39:42 2026 -0400

    feat(agent): add TopNPowerThreshold helper for retry-policy power bounds [ddx-67ed1062]
    
    Adds TopNPowerThreshold in cli/internal/agent/power_threshold.go.
    The function takes []agentlib.ModelInfo and a top-N count, returns the
    minimum power int that restricts routing to the top-N distinct power
    levels. Result is purely numeric — no model/provider/harness inspection.
    
    Tests cover: empty catalog, all-zero powers, top-1/2/3 thresholds,
    duplicate power dedup, N-exceeds-levels fallback, order stability, and
    a no-name-branching assertion.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 cli/internal/agent/power_threshold.go      | 51 ++++++++++++++++++
 cli/internal/agent/power_threshold_test.go | 86 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 137 insertions(+)

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"sort"
5	
6		agentlib "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent"
7	)
8	
9	// TopNPowerThreshold returns the minimum power value that, when used as
10	// MinPower, restricts routing to models within the top-n distinct power
11	// levels present in the catalog. Pass n=1 to allow only the highest-power
12	// models; n=2 to include the two strongest tiers, and so on.
13	//
14	// Returns 0 when models is empty or every entry carries a zero power value.
15	//
16	// The result depends only on power values; model IDs, provider names, and
17	// harness names are never inspected.
18	func TopNPowerThreshold(models []agentlib.ModelInfo, n int) int {
19		if len(models) == 0 || n <= 0 {
20			return 0
21		}
22	
23		// Collect non-zero power values (zero means unrated/unknown).
24		powers := make([]int, 0, len(models))
25		for _, m := range models {
26			if m.Power > 0 {
27				powers = append(powers, m.Power)
28			}
29		}
30		if len(powers) == 0 {
31			return 0
32		}
33	
34		// Sort descending so distinct-level extraction is a single pass.
35		sort.Sort(sort.Reverse(sort.IntSlice(powers)))
36	
37		// Build a deduplicated list of distinct power levels in descending order.
38		distinct := make([]int, 0, len(powers))
39		for _, p := range powers {
40			if len(distinct) == 0 || p != distinct[len(distinct)-1] {
41				distinct = append(distinct, p)
42			}
43		}
44	
45		// Return the n-th level (1-indexed). When fewer than n distinct levels
46		// exist, return the lowest level so the threshold remains satisfiable.
47		if n >= len(distinct) {
48			return distinct[len(distinct)-1]
49		}
50		return distinct[n-1]
51	}
52

18	
19	// failingReviewer always returns the given class as a typed review-error.
20	func failingReviewer(class string) BeadReviewerFunc {
21		return BeadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, resultRev, _, _ string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
22			return &ReviewResult{
23					Verdict:   VerdictBlock,
24					Error:     class,
25					ResultRev: resultRev,
26				}, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s: %w", class,
27					errors.New("simulated failure"))
28		})
29	}
30	
31	// seedReviewErrorEvents pre-populates a bead with N existing review-error
32	// events scoped to the given result_rev, simulating prior failed reviewer
33	// attempts. Used to drive the retry counter to its threshold without running
34	// the loop N-1 extra times.
35	func seedReviewErrorEvents(t *testing.T, store *bead.Store, beadID, resultRev, class string, n int) {
36		t.Helper()
37		for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
38			require.NoError(t, store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
39				Kind:      "review-error",
40				Summary:   class,
41				Body:      reviewErrorEventBody(class, i+1, resultRev, "prior failure"),
42				Actor:     "worker",
43				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
44				CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
45			}))
46		}
47	}
48	
49	// TestBoundedReviewRetry_NthFailureEmitsManualRequired covers FEAT-022 §14
50	// case (a): after N (default 3) review-error events on the same result_rev,
51	// the loop emits a terminal `review-manual-required` event and parks the
52	// bead so a subsequent execute-loop iteration does NOT re-execute primary.
53	func TestBoundedReviewRetry_NthFailureEmitsManualRequired(t *testing.T) {
54		store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
55		const resultRev = "deadbeef"
56	
57		// Seed N-1 prior failures (default N=3 → 2 prior events). The loop's
58		// reviewer attempt becomes the 3rd, which must trip the terminal event.
59		seedReviewErrorEvents(t, store, first.ID, resultRev, evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty, 2)
60	
61		var execCount atomic.Int32
62		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
63			Store: store,
64			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
65				if beadID == first.ID {
66					execCount.Add(1)
67				}
68				return ExecuteBeadReport{
69					BeadID:    beadID,
70					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
71					SessionID: "sess-r1",
72					ResultRev: resultRev,
73				}, nil
74			}),
75			Reviewer: failingReviewer(evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty),
76		}
77	
78		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
79		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
80		_, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{Once: true})
81		require.NoError(t, err)
82		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), execCount.Load(), "primary should run exactly once on this iteration")
83	
84		events, err := store.Events(first.ID)
85		require.NoError(t, err)
86	
87		var manual *bead.BeadEvent
88		for i := range events {
89			if events[i].Kind == "review-manual-required" {
90				manual = &events[i]
91				break
92			}
93		}
94		require.NotNil(t, manual, "Nth review failure must emit a terminal review-manual-required event")
95		assert.Contains(t, manual.Body, "failure_class="+evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty,
96			"manual-required body must include failure_class for triage")
97		assert.Contains(t, manual.Body, "attempt_count=3",
98			"manual-required body must include attempt_count for triage")
99		assert.Contains(t, manual.Body, "result_rev="+resultRev,
100			"manual-required body must include the blocked result_rev for triage")
101	
102		// §13 invariant: bead must NOT be closed by reviewer-failure escalation.
103		got, err := store.Get(first.ID)
104		require.NoError(t, err)
105		assert.NotEqual(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status,
106			"reviewer failure (even at terminal escalation) must never close the bead")
107	
108		// Subsequent execute-loop iteration must NOT re-execute primary. The
109		// bead is parked via SetExecutionCooldown so ReadyExecution skips it.
110		_, err = worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{Once: true})
111		require.NoError(t, err)
112		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), execCount.Load(),
113			"after review-manual-required the executor must not be invoked again")
114	}
115	
116	// TestBoundedReviewRetry_FreshResultRevResetsCounter covers FEAT-022 §14
117	// case (b): a new result_rev is a fresh review scope; the counter starts
118	// from zero even when the prior result_rev exhausted its budget.
119	func TestBoundedReviewRetry_FreshResultRevResetsCounter(t *testing.T) {
120		store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
121		const oldRev = "0000aaaa"
122		const newRev = "1111bbbb"
123	
124		// The previous result_rev exhausted its budget — N review-error events
125		// plus a review-manual-required event are present.
126		seedReviewErrorEvents(t, store, first.ID, oldRev, evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport, 3)
127		require.NoError(t, store.AppendEvent(first.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
128			Kind:    "review-manual-required",
129			Summary: evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport,
130			Body:    reviewErrorEventBody(evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport, 3, oldRev, "exhausted"),
131			Actor:   "worker",
132			Source:  "ddx agent execute-loop",
133		}))
134	
135		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
136			Store: store,
137			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
138				return ExecuteBeadReport{
139					BeadID:    beadID,
140					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
141					SessionID: "sess-fresh",
142					ResultRev: newRev,
143				}, nil
144			}),
145			Reviewer: failingReviewer(evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport),
146		}
147	
148		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
149		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
150		_, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{Once: true})
151		require.NoError(t, err)
152	
153		events, err := store.Events(first.ID)
154		require.NoError(t, err)
155	
156		// New scope: a single review-error event for the new result_rev with
157		// attempt_count=1. NO additional review-manual-required event (the
158		// original belongs to the old scope and must not be retriggered).
159		var freshErr *bead.BeadEvent
160		manualForNewRev := 0
161		for i := range events {
162			ev := events[i]
163			if ev.Kind == "review-error" && strings.Contains(ev.Body, "result_rev="+newRev) {
164				freshErr = &events[i]
165			}
166			if ev.Kind == "review-manual-required" && strings.Contains(ev.Body, "result_rev="+newRev) {
167				manualForNewRev++
168			}
169		}
170		require.NotNil(t, freshErr, "expected a fresh review-error event scoped to the new result_rev")
171		assert.Contains(t, freshErr.Body, "attempt_count=1",
172			"a new result_rev must reset the retry counter to 1")
173		assert.Equal(t, 0, manualForNewRev,
174			"a single failure under a fresh result_rev must not emit review-manual-required")
175	}

177	// TestBoundedReviewRetry_NoResultRevDoesNotConsumeBudget covers FEAT-022 §14
178	// case (c): iterations that do NOT produce a committed result_rev — execution
179	// failures and --no-merge runs (PreserveRef set, no merged ResultRev) — do
180	// not invoke the reviewer and therefore do not consume the retry budget.
181	func TestBoundedReviewRetry_NoResultRevDoesNotConsumeBudget(t *testing.T) {
182		t.Run("execution_failed", func(t *testing.T) {
183			store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
184	
185			var reviewerCalls atomic.Int32
186			reviewer := BeadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, _, _, _ string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
187				reviewerCalls.Add(1)

189			})
190	
191			worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
192				Store: store,
193				Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
194					return ExecuteBeadReport{
195						BeadID: beadID,
196						Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
197						Detail: "agent crashed",
198					}, nil
199				}),
200				Reviewer: reviewer,
201			}
202	
203			cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
204			rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
205			_, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{Once: true})
206			require.NoError(t, err)
207			assert.Equal(t, int32(0), reviewerCalls.Load(),

209	
210			events, err := store.Events(first.ID)

212			for _, ev := range events {
213				assert.NotEqual(t, "review-error", ev.Kind,
214					"execution_failed iterations must not append review-error events")
215				assert.NotEqual(t, "review-manual-required", ev.Kind,

5	// PushFailedReasonPrefix is the canonical Reason prefix written by the
6	// landing layer when `git push` rejects the new tip after a local merge or
7	// fast-forward succeeded. ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus matches on this prefix
8	// to map a merged-locally-but-not-pushed outcome to push_failed instead of
9	// success, so the bead stays open.
10	const PushFailedReasonPrefix = "landed locally; push failed:"
11	
12	// PushConflictReasonPrefix marks a more specific failure mode than
13	// PushFailedReasonPrefix: the local push was rejected because the remote
14	// advanced, the loop attempted an automatic pull/merge to recover, and the
15	// merge produced a conflict the loop cannot resolve. The bead is parked for
16	// human review with a short cooldown rather than a year-long park.
17	const PushConflictReasonPrefix = "landed locally; push conflict:"
18	
19	// FailureMode constants classify *why* an execution did not land cleanly.
20	// They are orthogonal to Outcome/Status: a single failed attempt carries one
21	// outcome (e.g. preserved) and one failure mode (e.g. merge_conflict) so
22	// measurement surfaces can aggregate failures by cause.
23	const (
24		FailureModeContextOverflow                = "context_overflow"
25		FailureModeMergeConflict                  = "merge_conflict"
26		FailureModeTestFailure                    = "test_failure"
27		FailureModeBuildFailure                   = "build_failure"
28		FailureModeTimeout                        = "timeout"
29		FailureModeAuthError                      = "auth_error"
30		FailureModeNoChanges                      = "no_changes"
31		FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced             = "no_evidence_produced"
32		FailureModeRatchetMiss                    = "ratchet_miss"
33		FailureModeNoViableProvider               = "no_viable_provider"
34		FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled            = "harness_not_installed"
35		FailureModeBlockedByPassthroughConstraint = "blocked_by_passthrough_constraint"
36		FailureModeAgentPowerUnsatisfied          = "agent_power_unsatisfied"
37		FailureModeUnknown                        = "unknown"
38	)

40	// ClassifyFailureMode derives a failure_mode for a worker-level result from
41	// its outcome, exit code, and error message. Returns "" when the outcome is
42	// task_succeeded (success carries no failure mode). task_no_changes always
43	// maps to FailureModeNoChanges regardless of error text.
44	//
45	// For task_failed (or non-zero exit code), the error message is scanned for
46	// well-known substrings. The order matters: context/auth/timeout patterns
47	// are checked before generic test/build patterns so a "test timed out"
48	// classifies as timeout rather than test_failure. Anything unrecognised
49	// maps to FailureModeUnknown so aggregates never hide a missed pattern.
50	func ClassifyFailureMode(outcome string, exitCode int, errMsg string) string {
51		switch outcome {
52		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
53			if exitCode == 0 {
54				return ""
55			}
56			// Degenerate: success outcome with non-zero exit. Treat as unknown
57			// failure rather than success so the aggregate flags the anomaly.
58		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
59			return FailureModeNoChanges
60		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
61			return FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced
62		}
63	
64		lower := strings.ToLower(errMsg)
65		switch {
66		case containsAny(lower,
67			"passthrough constraint unsatisfiable",
68			"passthrough constraint:",
69			"max_power is less than min_power",
70			"max_power exceeds min_power",
71			"harness cannot satisfy power constraint",
72			"harness pin incompatible with power bounds",
73			"model pin incompatible with power bounds",
74			"provider pin incompatible with power bounds"):
75			return FailureModeBlockedByPassthroughConstraint
76		case containsAny(lower,
77			"agent power unsatisfied",
78			"no model meets min_power",
79			"no model with power >=",
80			"minimum power not achievable",
81			"min_power constraint cannot be satisfied"):
82			return FailureModeAgentPowerUnsatisfied
83		case containsAny(lower,
84			"executable file not found in $path",
85			"executable file not found",
86			"no such file or directory") &&
87			containsAny(lower, "exec:", "harness", "claude", "agent", "codex", "gemini"):
88			return FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled
89		case containsAny(lower,
90			"no viable harness",
91			"no harness configured",
92			"failed to initialize routing service",
93			"no viable provider"):
94			return FailureModeNoViableProvider
95		case containsAny(lower,
96			"context length",
97			"context_length_exceeded",
98			"context window",
99			"maximum context",
100			"prompt is too long",
101			"prompt too long",
102			"token limit",
103			"too many tokens",
104			"exceeds the context"):
105			return FailureModeContextOverflow
106		case containsAny(lower,
107			"unauthorized",
108			"401 unauthorized",
109			"invalid api key",
110			"invalid_api_key",
111			"authentication failed",
112			"authentication_error",
113			"no api key",
114			"missing api key",
115			"quota exceeded",
116			"insufficient quota",
117			"insufficient_quota",
118			"rate limit",
119			"rate_limit",
120			"429"):
121			return FailureModeAuthError
122		case containsAny(lower,
123			"timeout",
124			"timed out",
125			"context deadline exceeded",
126			"deadline exceeded",
127			"signal: killed"):
128			return FailureModeTimeout
129		case containsAny(lower,
130			"merge conflict",
131			"ff-merge not possible",
132			"merge failed",
133			"automatic merge failed"):
134			return FailureModeMergeConflict
135		case containsAny(lower,
136			"build failed",
137			"compilation failed",
138			"compile error",
139			"compilation error",
140			"cannot find package",
141			"undefined:",
142			"undefined reference",
143			"syntax error"):
144			return FailureModeBuildFailure
145		case containsAny(lower,
146			"test failed",
147			"tests failed",
148			"--- fail:",
149			"assertion failed",
150			"test failure"):
151			return FailureModeTestFailure
152		}
153		if exitCode != 0 {
154			return FailureModeUnknown
155		}
156		return ""
157	}

159	// containsAny reports whether s contains any of the given substrings. s is
160	// assumed to already be lower-cased by the caller.
161	func containsAny(s string, needles ...string) bool {
162		for _, n := range needles {
163			if n == "" {
164				continue
165			}
166			if strings.Contains(s, n) {
167				return true
168			}
169		}
170		return false
171	}

173	// classifyLandingFailureMode computes the final failure_mode for a result
174	// after the orchestrator has made its landing decision. It prefers
175	// landing-specific signals (merge conflict, gate failure) over the worker's
176	// classification and falls back to workerMode for worker-level failures
177	// (agent execution failed, context overflow, etc.). Returns "" when the
178	// bead merged cleanly.
179	func classifyLandingFailureMode(landingOutcome, landingReason string, gateResults []GateCheckResult, workerMode string) string {
180		switch landingOutcome {
181		case "merged":
182			return ""
183		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
184			return FailureModeNoChanges
185		case "no-evidence", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
186			return FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced
187		case "error":
188			if workerMode != "" {
189				return workerMode
190			}
191			return FailureModeUnknown
192		case "preserved":
193			switch landingReason {
194			case "merge conflict", "merge failed", "ff-merge not possible":
195				return FailureModeMergeConflict
196			case "post-run checks failed":
197				return classifyGateFailure(gateResults)
198			case RatchetPreserveReason:
199				return FailureModeRatchetMiss
200			}
201			// Other preserved reasons (e.g. "agent execution failed",
202			// "--no-merge specified") defer to the worker's classification.
203			if workerMode != "" {
204				return workerMode
205			}
206			return FailureModeUnknown
207		}
208		if workerMode != "" {
209			return workerMode
210		}
211		return FailureModeUnknown
212	}

214	// classifyGateFailure inspects failed gate results to distinguish
215	// build_failure from test_failure. Gate definitions that fail to compile
216	// before running (go build, tsc, cargo build) typically surface build
217	// diagnostics in stderr; test runners surface test failure markers. When
218	// ambiguous, defaults to test_failure since the post-run checks gate is
219	// test-oriented by convention.
220	func classifyGateFailure(gateResults []GateCheckResult) string {
221		for _, gr := range gateResults {
222			if gr.Status != "fail" {
223				continue
224			}
225			combined := strings.ToLower(gr.Stdout + "\n" + gr.Stderr)
226			if containsAny(combined,
227				"build failed",
228				"compilation failed",
229				"compile error",
230				"compilation error",
231				"cannot find package",
232				"undefined:",
233				"undefined reference",
234				"syntax error") {
235				return FailureModeBuildFailure
236			}
237		}
238		return FailureModeTestFailure
239	}

267		// ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition is a structured outcome
268		// signalling that the executor inspected the bead and concluded it cannot

275		// with a long cooldown and records the recommended sub-beads as a
276		// structured `decomposition-recommendation` event. No further attempts
277		// happen until an operator clears the cooldown (e.g. via
278		// `ddx bead cooldown clear`) or splits the bead.
279		ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition = "declined_needs_decomposition"

284		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock          = "review_block"
285		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction    = "review_malfunction"

287		// ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable is set when execute-loop

290		// The bead is parked under LandConflictCooldown for operator review.
291		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable = "land_conflict_unresolvable"

300	// ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus maps a landing outcome to the supervisor-visible
301	// status contract. This is called by ApplyLandingToResult and by callers who
302	// build an ExecuteBeadReport from a landing result.
303	func ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(outcome string, exitCode int, reason string) string {
304		if exitCode != 0 {
305			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
306		}
307	
308		switch outcome {
309		case "merged":
310			// A merged outcome whose reason carries the push-failure marker
311			// means the local target branch advanced but `git push` rejected the
312			// new tip (large blob, branch protection, auth, divergence, etc.).
313			// Classify these as push_failed so the loop refuses to close the
314			// bead and operators are forced to investigate.
315			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushConflictReasonPrefix) {
316				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict
317			}
318			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushFailedReasonPrefix) {
319				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed
320			}
321			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
322		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
323			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
324		case "no-evidence", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
325			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced
326		case "error":
327			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
328		case "preserved":
329			switch reason {
330			case "merge conflict", "merge failed", "ff-merge not possible":
331				return ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict
332			case "post-run checks failed":
333				return ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed
334			case RatchetPreserveReason:
335				return ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed
336			default:
337				if isPreservedNeedsReviewReason(reason) {
338					return ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
339				}
340				// Preserved iterations may still be success when the caller
341				// explicitly requested no merge.
342				return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
343			}
344		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
345			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
346		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed:
347			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
348		default:
349			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
350		}
351	}
352	
353	func isPreservedNeedsReviewReason(reason string) bool {
354		for _, prefix := range []string{
355			"large-deletion gate:",
356			"syntax sanity gate:",
357			"post-land gate failed:",
358		} {
359			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, prefix) {
360				return true
361			}
362		}
363		return false
364	}
365	
366	func ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(status, reason, errMsg string) string {
367		switch {
368		case reason != "" && errMsg != "" && reason != errMsg:
369			return reason + ": " + errMsg
370		case reason != "":
371			return reason
372		case errMsg != "":
373			return errMsg
374		case status != "":
375			return status
376		default:
377			return "unknown"
378		}
379	}

23	// ExecuteBeadResult captures the outcome of an execute-bead worker run.
24	// The worker populates the task-level fields (BeadID through UsageFile).
25	// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult) populates the landing fields

27	// ChecksFile) via ApplyLandingToResult before the result is written to disk or
28	// returned to a caller.

117	// GitOps abstracts the git operations required by the worker.
118	// Merge is intentionally excluded — that belongs to the parent-side
119	// orchestrator (OrchestratorGitOps). UpdateRef/DeleteRef are exposed here
120	// so landing-side helpers (e.g. BuildLandingGateContext) can pin a
121	// transient ref while running gate evaluation against an ephemeral
122	// worktree.
123	type GitOps interface {
124		HeadRev(dir string) (string, error)
125		ResolveRev(dir, rev string) (string, error)
126		WorktreeAdd(dir, wtPath, rev string) error
127		WorktreeRemove(dir, wtPath string) error
128		WorktreeList(dir string) ([]string, error)
129		WorktreePrune(dir string) error
130		IsDirty(dir string) (bool, error)
131		// SynthesizeCommit stages real file changes (excluding harness noise paths) and
132		// commits them using msg as the commit message. Returns (true, nil) when a
133		// commit was made, (false, nil) when there was nothing real to commit (all
134		// dirty files were noise), and (false, err) on failure.
135		SynthesizeCommit(dir, msg string) (bool, error)
136		// UpdateRef updates ref in dir to sha. Used by landing helpers to pin a
137		// commit so a transient worktree can check it out without racing with
138		// other work that might prune it.
139		UpdateRef(dir, ref, sha string) error
140		// DeleteRef removes ref from dir. Used to unpin a transient ref after
141		// the consumer (e.g. an ephemeral worktree) is done with it.
142		DeleteRef(dir, ref string) error
143	}

5	// Bead represents a portable work item with metadata.
6	// The schema matches bd/br JSONL format for interchange compatibility.
7	// Unknown fields from external sources are preserved in Extra.
8	type Bead struct {
9		ID        string    `json:"id"`
10		Title     string    `json:"title"`
11		Status    string    `json:"status"`
12		Priority  int       `json:"priority"`
13		IssueType string    `json:"issue_type"`
14		Owner     string    `json:"owner,omitempty"`
15		CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
16		CreatedBy string    `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
17		UpdatedAt time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
18	
19		// Optional fields (bd-compatible)
20		Labels      []string `json:"labels,omitempty"`
21		Parent      string   `json:"parent,omitempty"`
22		Description string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
23		Acceptance  string   `json:"acceptance,omitempty"`
24		Notes       string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
25	
26		// Dependencies use bd-compatible format
27		Dependencies []Dependency `json:"dependencies,omitempty"`
28	
29		// Extra holds unknown fields for round-trip preservation.
30		// Workflow-specific fields (e.g. HELIX spec-id, execution-eligible)
31		// are stored here and written back on save.
32		Extra map[string]any `json:"-"`
33	}

36	type Dependency struct {
37		IssueID     string `json:"issue_id"`
38		DependsOnID string `json:"depends_on_id"`
39		Type        string `json:"type"` // "blocks", "related", etc.
40		CreatedAt   string `json:"created_at,omitempty"`

110	// DepIDs returns a flat list of dependency IDs for this bead.
111	func (b *Bead) DepIDs() []string {
112		var ids []string
113		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
114			ids = append(ids, d.DependsOnID)
115		}
116		return ids
117	}
118	
119	// HasDep returns true if the bead depends on the given ID.
120	func (b *Bead) HasDep(id string) bool {
121		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
122			if d.DependsOnID == id {
123				return true
124			}
125		}
126		return false
127	}
128	
129	// AddDep adds a dependency if it doesn't already exist.
130	func (b *Bead) AddDep(depID, depType string) {
131		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
132			if d.DependsOnID == depID {
133				return // already exists
134			}
135		}
136		b.Dependencies = append(b.Dependencies, Dependency{
137			IssueID:     b.ID,
138			DependsOnID: depID,
139			Type:        depType,
140			CreatedAt:   time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
141		})
142	}
143	
144	// RemoveDep removes a dependency by target ID.
145	func (b *Bead) RemoveDep(depID string) {
146		var filtered []Dependency
147		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
148			if d.DependsOnID != depID {
149				filtered = append(filtered, d)
150			}
151		}
152		b.Dependencies = filtered
153	}

11	# Feature: Prompt Evidence Assembly and Size Invariants
12	
13	**ID:** FEAT-022
14	**Status:** Implemented
15	**Priority:** P1
16	**Owner:** DDx Team
17	
18	## Overview
19	
20	DDx constructs prompts for LLM agents from multiple evidence sources: bead
21	metadata, governing documents, git diffs, comparison-arm outputs, user-supplied
22	prompt files, and inline request bodies. Today these assembly paths inline
23	content without size bounds, produce oversize prompts, and in the reviewer's
24	case trigger silent provider-side failures that cascade into redone primary
25	work and wasted tokens.
26	
27	This feature establishes a single repo-wide invariant: **no prompt DDx
28	constructs or accepts may have unbounded size.** It specifies the shared
29	primitives every prompt-building call site must use, the two supported
30	assembly strategies, the failure semantics when inputs exceed caps, and the
31	telemetry needed to observe the system.
32	
33	## Problem Statement
34	
35	**Current situation:**
36	
37	- The post-merge bead reviewer (`BuildReviewPrompt`) inlines full governing
38	  documents and full `git show` diffs without a cap. Large diffs push the
39	  prompt past the reviewer model's context window; some providers return a
40	  hard error, some return zero bytes. DDx surfaces these as `review-error`,
41	  fails to close the bead, and the next worker redoes the primary task —
42	  burning the same large token budget 3–4 times per bead before an operator
43	  intervenes.
44	- The comparison grader (`buildGradingPrompt`) has the same failure shape:
45	  per-arm outputs and diffs are inlined without a cap.
46	- A second review-prompt assembler lives in `cmd/bead_review.go` as
47	  duplicated code, with divergent escape semantics from the agent-side
48	  assembler.
49	- Five call sites (`runner.go`, `compare_adapter.go:181`,
50	  `compare_adapter.go:614`, `service_run.go:83`, `execute_bead.go:1291`)
51	  read `--prompt` files with `os.ReadFile` and no cap. A 100 MB prompt file
52	  reaches the provider unchecked.
53	- Three inline prompt-ingress paths (`/api/agent/run` body `Prompt`,
54	  `DDX_AGENT_PROMPT` env in exec definitions, docgraph prompt metadata)
55	  accept prompt bodies with no cap.
56	- Server text egress (MCP, REST, GraphQL) returns full document, diff,
57	  session, and persona payloads without caps. Any of these responses may
58	  later flow back into a prompt.
59	- No DDx call site has section-level telemetry on prompt assembly. When
60	  reviews fail, diagnosis today requires per-execution bundle archaeology.
61	
62	**Desired outcome:** Every LLM prompt DDx constructs, accepts, or emits
63	through text-returning server endpoints is bounded by explicit caps.
64	Prompt-assembly call sites use shared primitives that enforce caps with
65	clear truncation markers. Oversize inputs fail fast with actionable errors
66	rather than silently corrupting provider calls. Operators see per-section
67	byte accounting on every review and grading attempt.
68	
69	## Requirements
70	
71	### Functional
72	
73	**Shared evidence primitives**
74	
75	1. **Single evidence-assembly package.** DDx provides one package exporting
76	   the primitives every prompt-building call site must use. The package
77	   covers, at minimum, these capabilities:
78	   - Byte-size caps for inlined files, diffs, governing documents, and
79	     total prompts, each with per-harness override resolution.
80	   - Clamped file read (returns content plus amount truncated).
81	   - Clamped text output (bounded length with a canonical truncation
82	     marker).
83	   - Section fitting under a total byte budget with priority-ordered
84	     inclusion. Default behavior is line-based and type-agnostic;
85	     markdown-aware heading extraction is an optional secondary mode.
86	   - Diff clamping and diff decomposition. The decomposer returns file
87	     inventory, per-file stat lines, and hunk headers so large diffs
88	     can degrade to stat + hunk-headers only.
89	   - Two strategy helpers, one for inline-with-cap assembly and one for
90	     ref-only metadata assembly (see §2).
91	   - A canonical truncation-marker string so downstream parsers
92	     recognize truncation uniformly across call sites.
93	
94	   Specific Go identifiers, type shapes, and constant values are defined
95	   in the solution design document for this feature, not in this spec.
96	
97	1a. **Cap defaults and configurability.** Byte-size caps have conservative
98	    defaults shipped in the binary. Project-level overrides are expressed
99	    in `.ddx/config.yaml` under a dedicated evidence-caps section;
100	    per-harness overrides resolve from the same configuration file.
101	    Defaults and override precedence are defined in the solution design
102	    for this feature. The invariant is that every cap is configurable at
103	    the project level without a rebuild; specific numeric defaults are
104	    not frozen by this spec.
105	
106	2. **Two supported assembly strategies.** Each call site must adopt one:
107	   - **Ref-only metadata** — the primary bead prompt's pattern. Paths and
108	     titles only; agent reads file content via its own tools. Appropriate
109	     when the agent has filesystem access and the prompt does not need to
110	     guarantee the agent saw specific content.
111	   - **Inline-with-cap** — reviewer and grader's pattern. Content is
112	     inlined with bounded size, priority-ordered sections, and truncation
113	     markers. Appropriate when the call must guarantee the agent saw
114	     specific evidence.
115	
116	3. **Repo-wide invariant (no unbounded prompts).** No code path may produce
117	   a value that ends up as an agent-invocation prompt, as a text response
118	   from a server handler that carries library, execution, diff, session,
119	   or persona content, or as an inline prompt body accepted through any
120	   ingress surface, without going through an evidence-package primitive.
121	   Exceptions require an explicit `evidence:allow-unbounded` source
122	   annotation with a justifying comment and are enforced by lint (see
123	   Non-Functional §Lint). Static/literal prompt fragments compiled into
124	   the binary are exempt; the invariant governs dynamic assembly from
125	   runtime inputs, not constant strings.
126	
127	**Bounded prompt construction**
128	
129	4. **Single review assembler.** Review prompts are assembled by exactly one
130	   function (`agent.BuildReviewPrompt`). Any duplicate review-prompt
131	   assembler in the CLI command layer is collapsed into this function.
132	   Divergent escape, instruction-template, or section-ordering logic is
133	   reconciled with byte-equivalence tests before the duplicate is removed.
134	
135	5. **Review prompt contents.** The review prompt includes, in priority
136	   order:
137	   1. Bead metadata + acceptance criteria + bead notes (minimum evidence
138	      floor — always present regardless of budget).
139	   2. Changed-file inventory (names + stat lines — minimum evidence floor).
140	   3. Governing document content, bounded per §1. Governing docs that
141	      exceed their cap degrade to heading outline + first N lines.
142	   4. Diff, ranked by relevance (files named in AC text > files matching
143	      governing ref paths > others); per-file cap applies; large files
144	      degrade to stat + hunk headers.
145	
146	6. **Grading prompt contents.** The grading prompt follows the same
147	   bounded-assembly pattern and additionally includes, bounded via
148	   `ClampOutput`, each comparison arm's `PostRunOut`, `PostRunOK`, and
149	   `ToolCalls`. These are first-class evidence fields on the comparison
150	   arm type and must not be silently dropped.
151	
152	7. **Pre-dispatch short-circuit.** When a bounded assembly still exceeds
153	   `MaxPromptBytes` after all trimming, the call site must not dispatch
154	   the provider. Reviewer paths emit a distinct
155	   `review-error: context_overflow` outcome (see §13).
156	
157	**Bounded prompt ingress**
158	
159	8. **Hard-fail on oversize `--prompt` files.** Every call site that reads a
160	   user-supplied prompt file uses `evidence.ReadFileClamped(MaxPromptBytes)`
161	   and hard-fails on oversize with an actionable error message that
162	   includes observed size and cap. Silent truncation is forbidden — a
163	   user-supplied prompt truncated without notice produces incorrect
164	   results the user cannot diagnose.
165	
166	9. **Hard-fail on oversize inline prompt bodies.** The same hard-fail
167	   applies to inline prompt bodies in `/api/agent/run` request bodies,
168	   `DDX_AGENT_PROMPT` environment values, and any other ingress path that
169	   accepts a raw prompt string. Size is checked against `MaxPromptBytes`
170	   before the prompt is handed to the agent.
171	
172	**Bounded server text egress**
173	
174	10. **Universal bounded text responses.** Every server text-returning
175	    handler — MCP tools, REST endpoints, GraphQL resolvers — that emits
176	    library document content, execution bundle content, diff content,
177	    session content, or persona content applies `ClampOutput` and marks
178	    the response as truncated in the response structure when trimming
179	    occurred. The MCP tool response structure, the REST JSON response,
180	    and the GraphQL resolver return type each carry an explicit
181	    `truncated: bool` and `original_bytes` field where applicable.
182	
183	11. **No prompt-adjacent surface is unbounded.** The invariant applies
184	    regardless of whether a specific response is, today, actually consumed
185	    back into a prompt. A bounded-by-default policy prevents new
186	    consumers from reintroducing the overflow class.
187	
188	**Error classes and review policy**
189	
190	12. **Review error taxonomy.** The reviewer outcome event distinguishes
191	    four classes:
192	    - `review-error: context_overflow` — bounded assembly exceeded
193	      `MaxPromptBytes` post-trim; provider was not called.
194	    - `review-error: provider_empty` — provider returned zero bytes.
195	    - `review-error: unparseable` — provider returned text but no
196	      recognizable verdict line.
197	    - `review-error: transport` — network or provider error.
198	
199	13. **Reviewer failure preserves the gate.** Reviewer failure must not
200	    close the bead. This invariant (enforced today by
201	    `execute_bead_review_failure_modes_test.go`) is preserved: bounded
202	    prompts change how the reviewer is called, not whether a failed
203	    review can auto-approve.
204	
205	14. **Bounded review retry.** Reviewer invocations are capped at a
206	    configurable N attempts per `result_rev`. On the Nth failure, DDx
207	    emits a terminal `review-manual-required` event and stops re-
208	    executing primary. A new `result_rev` (fresh primary attempt after
209	    manual operator action) resets the counter. Iterations that do not
210	    produce a `result_rev` (e.g. `--no-merge` runs, execution-failed
211	    outcomes) do not consume the review-retry budget; the counter is
212	    scoped strictly to reviewer attempts against a committed result.
213	    This bounds the cost-bleed pattern observed in the filed bug (same
214	    primary task retried 3–4 times) without weakening the gate.
215	
216	**Telemetry and observability**
217	
218	15. **Section-level assembly telemetry.** Review and grading paths
219	    extend the execute-bead attempt bundle (FEAT-014 §19/§20, FEAT-005
220	    §Execute-Bead Evidence Bundle) with an evidence-assembly block
221	    containing:
222	    - per-section bytes (bead / governing / diff / arm-output)
223	    - list of selected vs omitted governing refs
224	    - list of selected vs omitted diff files
225	    - truncation reasons per section
226	    - total `input_bytes` and `output_bytes`
227	    - `elapsed_ms`, `harness`, `model`
228	
229	    Runtime metrics fields already defined by FEAT-014 (`harness`,
230	    `model`, `session_id`, `elapsed_ms`, `input_tokens`,
231	    `output_tokens`, `total_tokens`, `cost_usd`, `base_rev`,
232	    `result_rev`) are not duplicated by FEAT-022; evidence-assembly
233	    telemetry is additive to that record, not a replacement.
234	
235	16. **Compact event summary.** `review`, `review-error`, and
236	    `compare-result` event bodies carry a compact summary
237	    (`input_bytes`, `output_bytes`, `elapsed_ms`, `harness`, `model`).
238	    Full section detail stays in artifacts; event bodies respect the

241	17. **Metrics surface.** DDx's existing review-outcomes metrics surface
242	    exposes prompt-size quantiles and the four-class failure breakdown
243	    defined in §12. The specific CLI and API entry points that carry
244	    this data are inherited from FEAT-006 and FEAT-014; FEAT-022 adds
245	    fields, not new commands.

247	### Non-Functional
248	
249	- **Lint (structural sink coverage).** A vet/lint rule, scoped
250	  structurally not by dataflow, enforces the invariant in §3. Rule keys:
251	  - assignment to `RunOptions.Prompt`
252	  - construction of `mcpContent{Type:"text", ...}` literals
253	  - server handlers that write text response bodies from library,
254	    execution, diff, session, or persona data
255	  - readers of `opts.PromptFile` or inline prompt-body request fields
256	  Any flagged path must route through an `internal/evidence` primitive

260	- **Byte-based enforcement and calibration.** All caps are enforced in
261	  bytes. Token-based caps are deferred until DDx has a per-harness
262	  tokenizer binding. Default byte caps are calibrated conservatively
263	  relative to the smallest common-reviewer model's context window using
264	  an assumed worst-case bytes-per-token ratio; the solution design
265	  document carries the concrete calibration table and the per-harness
266	  override mechanism (see §1a). If runtime telemetry (§15) shows that
267	  the default is either rejecting valid prompts or failing to prevent

270	
271	- **Failure mode clarity.** Oversize inputs produce errors that name the
272	  input source, the observed size, and the cap. Empty provider responses

274	
275	- **Backward compatibility.** Bead 0 primitives ship with permissive
276	  default caps and cause no behavioral change on land. Tightening caps
277	  and consuming the primitives happens in subsequent beads; each
278	  consumer change is independently reviewable.
279	
280	- **Performance.** Assembly cost per call is bounded by total prompt
281	  size (`O(MaxPromptBytes)`), not by input size on disk. Clamping
282	  occurs at read time; oversize files are not fully loaded into memory.

287	**As** an operator running `ddx work` against beads with large diffs

289	acceptable diff, and to fail with an actionable overflow error otherwise
290	**So that** primary work is not silently redone after empty reviewer
291	responses

293	**Acceptance Criteria:**

299	  receives a `review-error: context_overflow` event with observed and

58		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:

60		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
61			return FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced
62		}
63	
64		lower := strings.ToLower(errMsg)
65		switch {
66		case containsAny(lower,
67			"passthrough constraint unsatisfiable",
68			"passthrough constraint:",
69			"max_power is less than min_power",
70			"max_power exceeds min_power",
71			"harness cannot satisfy power constraint",
72			"harness pin incompatible with power bounds",
73			"model pin incompatible with power bounds",
74			"provider pin incompatible with power bounds"):
75			return FailureModeBlockedByPassthroughConstraint
76		case containsAny(lower,
77			"agent power unsatisfied",
78			"no model meets min_power",
79			"no model with power >=",
80			"minimum power not achievable",
81			"min_power constraint cannot be satisfied"):
82			return FailureModeAgentPowerUnsatisfied
83		case containsAny(lower,
84			"executable file not found in $path",
85			"executable file not found",
86			"no such file or directory") &&
87			containsAny(lower, "exec:", "harness", "claude", "agent", "codex", "gemini"):
88			return FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled
89		case containsAny(lower,
90			"no viable harness",
91			"no harness configured",
92			"failed to initialize routing service",
93			"no viable provider"):
94			return FailureModeNoViableProvider
95		case containsAny(lower,
96			"context length",
97			"context_length_exceeded",
98			"context window",
99			"maximum context",
100			"prompt is too long",
101			"prompt too long",
102			"token limit",
103			"too many tokens",
104			"exceeds the context"):
105			return FailureModeContextOverflow
106		case containsAny(lower,
107			"unauthorized",
108			"401 unauthorized",
109			"invalid api key",
110			"invalid_api_key",
111			"authentication failed",
112			"authentication_error",
113			"no api key",
114			"missing api key",
115			"quota exceeded",
116			"insufficient quota",
117			"insufficient_quota",
118			"rate limit",
119			"rate_limit",
120			"429"):
121			return FailureModeAuthError
122		case containsAny(lower,
123			"timeout",
124			"timed out",
125			"context deadline exceeded",
126			"deadline exceeded",
127			"signal: killed"):
128			return FailureModeTimeout
129		case containsAny(lower,
130			"merge conflict",
131			"ff-merge not possible",
132			"merge failed",
133			"automatic merge failed"):
134			return FailureModeMergeConflict
135		case containsAny(lower,
136			"build failed",
137			"compilation failed",
138			"compile error",
139			"compilation error",
140			"cannot find package",
141			"undefined:",
142			"undefined reference",
143			"syntax error"):
144			return FailureModeBuildFailure
145		case containsAny(lower,
146			"test failed",
147			"tests failed",
148			"--- fail:",
149			"assertion failed",
150			"test failure"):
151			return FailureModeTestFailure
152		}
153		if exitCode != 0 {
154			return FailureModeUnknown
155		}
156		return ""
157	}

159	// containsAny reports whether s contains any of the given substrings. s is
160	// assumed to already be lower-cased by the caller.
161	func containsAny(s string, needles ...string) bool {
162		for _, n := range needles {
163			if n == "" {
164				continue
165			}
166			if strings.Contains(s, n) {
167				return true
168			}
169		}
170		return false
171	}

173	// classifyLandingFailureMode computes the final failure_mode for a result
174	// after the orchestrator has made its landing decision. It prefers
175	// landing-specific signals (merge conflict, gate failure) over the worker's
176	// classification and falls back to workerMode for worker-level failures
177	// (agent execution failed, context overflow, etc.). Returns "" when the
178	// bead merged cleanly.
179	func classifyLandingFailureMode(landingOutcome, landingReason string, gateResults []GateCheckResult, workerMode string) string {
180		switch landingOutcome {
181		case "merged":
182			return ""
183		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
184			return FailureModeNoChanges
185		case "no-evidence", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
186			return FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced
187		case "error":
188			if workerMode != "" {
189				return workerMode
190			}
191			return FailureModeUnknown
192		case "preserved":
193			switch landingReason {
194			case "merge conflict", "merge failed", "ff-merge not possible":
195				return FailureModeMergeConflict
196			case "post-run checks failed":
197				return classifyGateFailure(gateResults)
198			case RatchetPreserveReason:
199				return FailureModeRatchetMiss
200			}
201			// Other preserved reasons (e.g. "agent execution failed",
202			// "--no-merge specified") defer to the worker's classification.
203			if workerMode != "" {
204				return workerMode
205			}
206			return FailureModeUnknown
207		}
208		if workerMode != "" {
209			return workerMode
210		}
211		return FailureModeUnknown
212	}
213	
214	// classifyGateFailure inspects failed gate results to distinguish
215	// build_failure from test_failure. Gate definitions that fail to compile
216	// before running (go build, tsc, cargo build) typically surface build
217	// diagnostics in stderr; test runners surface test failure markers. When
218	// ambiguous, defaults to test_failure since the post-run checks gate is
219	// test-oriented by convention.
220	func classifyGateFailure(gateResults []GateCheckResult) string {
221		for _, gr := range gateResults {
222			if gr.Status != "fail" {
223				continue
224			}
225			combined := strings.ToLower(gr.Stdout + "\n" + gr.Stderr)
226			if containsAny(combined,
227				"build failed",
228				"compilation failed",
229				"compile error",
230				"compilation error",
231				"cannot find package",
232				"undefined:",
233				"undefined reference",
234				"syntax error") {
235				return FailureModeBuildFailure
236			}
237		}
238		return FailureModeTestFailure
239	}

246		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed     = "task_failed"
247		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges  = "task_no_changes"

251	// Status constants — used by the loop and other consumers of ExecuteBeadReport.
252	// These map to specific close/unclaim behaviors in ExecuteBeadWorker.
253	const (
254		ExecuteBeadStatusStructuralValidationFailed = "structural_validation_failed"
255		ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed            = "execution_failed"
256		ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed         = "post_run_check_failed"
257		ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed              = "ratchet_failed"
258		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict               = "land_conflict"
259		ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed                 = "push_failed"
260		ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict               = "push_conflict"
261		ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview       = "preserved_needs_review"
262		ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced         = "no_evidence_produced"
263		ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges                  = "no_changes"
264		ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied           = "already_satisfied"

267		// ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition is a structured outcome
268		// signalling that the executor inspected the bead and concluded it cannot
269		// be delivered in a single pass — typically because the scope is
270		// epic-sized and must first be broken into sub-beads. It is distinct
271		// from no_changes (which means "I tried and there was nothing to change")
272		// and from execution_failed (which means the agent or harness errored).
273		//
274		// The loop treats this status as terminal-for-loop: it parks the bead
275		// with a long cooldown and records the recommended sub-beads as a
276		// structured `decomposition-recommendation` event. No further attempts
277		// happen until an operator clears the cooldown (e.g. via
278		// `ddx bead cooldown clear`) or splits the bead.
279		ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition = "declined_needs_decomposition"
280	
281		// Post-merge review outcomes. The bead was merged, then reviewed;
282		// the review returned a non-APPROVE verdict and the bead was reopened.
283		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges = "review_request_changes"
284		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock          = "review_block"
285		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction    = "review_malfunction"
286	
287		// ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable is set when execute-loop
288		// attempted 3-way ort auto-resolution and (if configured) a focused
289		// conflict-resolve agent run for a preserved iteration, and both failed.
290		// The bead is parked under LandConflictCooldown for operator review.
291		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable = "land_conflict_unresolvable"
292	
293		// ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictNeedsHuman is set when the focused
294		// conflict-resolve agent escalated to BLOCKING — the conflict requires
295		// human judgment, not automated retry. Parked under the same short
296		// cooldown with a kind:land-conflict-needs-human event.
297		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictNeedsHuman = "land_conflict_needs_human"
298	)

300	// ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus maps a landing outcome to the supervisor-visible
301	// status contract. This is called by ApplyLandingToResult and by callers who
302	// build an ExecuteBeadReport from a landing result.
303	func ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(outcome string, exitCode int, reason string) string {
304		if exitCode != 0 {
305			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
306		}
307	
308		switch outcome {
309		case "merged":
310			// A merged outcome whose reason carries the push-failure marker
311			// means the local target branch advanced but `git push` rejected the
312			// new tip (large blob, branch protection, auth, divergence, etc.).
313			// Classify these as push_failed so the loop refuses to close the
314			// bead and operators are forced to investigate.
315			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushConflictReasonPrefix) {
316				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict
317			}
318			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushFailedReasonPrefix) {
319				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed
320			}
321			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
322		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
323			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
324		case "no-evidence", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
325			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced
326		case "error":
327			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
328		case "preserved":
329			switch reason {
330			case "merge conflict", "merge failed", "ff-merge not possible":
331				return ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict
332			case "post-run checks failed":
333				return ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed
334			case RatchetPreserveReason:
335				return ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed
336			default:
337				if isPreservedNeedsReviewReason(reason) {
338					return ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
339				}
340				// Preserved iterations may still be success when the caller
341				// explicitly requested no merge.
342				return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
343			}
344		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
345			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
346		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed:
347			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
348		default:
349			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
350		}
351	}
352	
353	func isPreservedNeedsReviewReason(reason string) bool {
354		for _, prefix := range []string{
355			"large-deletion gate:",
356			"syntax sanity gate:",
357			"post-land gate failed:",
358		} {
359			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, prefix) {
360				return true
361			}
362		}
363		return false
364	}
365	
366	func ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(status, reason, errMsg string) string {
367		switch {
368		case reason != "" && errMsg != "" && reason != errMsg:
369			return reason + ": " + errMsg
370		case reason != "":
371			return reason
372		case errMsg != "":
373			return errMsg
374		case status != "":
375			return status
376		default:
377			return "unknown"
378		}
379	}

46	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
47	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
48	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
49	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
50	
51	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
52	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
53	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
54	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
55	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
56	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
57	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
58	
59	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
60	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
61	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
62	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
63	// advance the base branch.
64	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
65	
66	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
67	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
68	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
69	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
70	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
71	// worker.
72	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
73	
74	// handleOutcomeStoreError is called when a Store.* operation in the
75	// post-Execute outcome block returns a non-nil error. It logs the error,
76	// appends a kind:loop-error event to the bead (best-effort), sets a short
77	// cooldown so the bead is not immediately re-queued, increments
78	// result.Failures, and returns true — signalling the caller to continue to
79	// the next loop iteration rather than returning.
80	//
81	// If ctx is already cancelled, it returns false so the caller can propagate
82	// the cancellation immediately.
83	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx context.Context, beadID, operation string, storeErr error, assignee string, result *ExecuteBeadLoopResult, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, now func() time.Time) bool {
84		if ctx.Err() != nil {
85			return false
86		}
87		if runtime.Log != nil {
88			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "outcome store error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", operation, beadID, storeErr)
89		}
90		_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
91			Kind:      "loop-error",
92			Summary:   operation + " failed",
93			Body:      storeErr.Error(),
94			Actor:     assignee,
95			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
96			CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
97		})
98		cooldownUntil := now().UTC().Add(StoreErrorCooldown)
99		_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(beadID, cooldownUntil, "loop-error", operation+": "+storeErr.Error())
100		result.Failures++
101		result.LastFailureStatus = "loop-error"
102		return true
103	}
104	
105	// capLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
106	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
107	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
108	func capLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
109		if d <= 0 {
110			return 0
111		}
112		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
113			return MaxLoopCooldown
114		}
115		return d
116	}

267	// ExecuteBeadWorker drains the current single-project execution-ready queue.
268	// It intentionally does not retry a failed/conflicted bead again in the same
269	// process run; a later operator-driven invocation can create the next attempt.
270	type ExecuteBeadWorker struct {
271		Store               ExecuteBeadLoopStore
272		Executor            ExecuteBeadExecutor
273		SatisfactionChecker SatisfactionChecker // nil → count-based default
274		Now                 func() time.Time
275		// Reviewer, when non-nil, is called after every successful merge to
276		// validate the commit against the bead's acceptance criteria. When nil,
277		// post-merge review is skipped (same behaviour as --no-review).
278		Reviewer BeadReviewer
279	
280		// ConflictResolver, when non-nil, is called after the 3-way ort auto-merge
281		// step fails to recover a preserved iteration. The implementation should
282		// attempt a focused conflict-resolution pass (e.g. a cheap-tier agent run
283		// with the conflict files and bead AC) and return the new merged tip SHA on
284		// success. isBlocking=true signals the conflict requires human judgment
285		// (escalating to land_conflict_needs_human); false means failed-but-retriable.
286		ConflictResolver func(ctx context.Context, beadID, preserveRef, projectRoot string) (newTip string, isBlocking bool, err error)
287	
288		// ComplexityGate, when non-nil, is invoked between RoutePreflight and Claim.
289		// It evaluates whether the candidate bead is atomic (proceed to Claim),
290		// decomposable (file children, block parent, re-pick), or ambiguous
291		// (surface to human triage). When nil, the gate is bypassed and a one-time
292		// warning is emitted per Run() invocation. See triage.go and
293		// NewComplexityGate for the standard implementation.
294		ComplexityGate TriageGate
295	
296		// conflictAutoRecoverFn replaces the default landConflictAutoRecover. Set
297		// in tests to inject controlled recovery results without a real git repo.
298		conflictAutoRecoverFn func(wd, preserveRef string, gitOps LandingGitOps) (string, error)
299	}

303	func emitProgress(ch chan<- ProgressEvent, evt ProgressEvent) {
304		if ch == nil {
305			return
306		}
307		select {
308		case ch <- evt:
309		default:
310		}
311	}
