335	
336	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) Run(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error) {
337		if w.Store == nil {
338			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: store is required")
339		}
340		if w.Executor == nil {
341			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: executor is required")
342		}
343	
344		now := w.Now
345		if now == nil {
346			now = time.Now
347		}
348		assignee := rcfg.Assignee()
349		if assignee == "" {
350			assignee = "ddx"
351		}
352		noProgressCooldown := rcfg.NoProgressCooldown()
353		if noProgressCooldown <= 0 {
354			noProgressCooldown = 6 * time.Hour
355		}
356		maxNoChangesBeforeClose := rcfg.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose()
357		if maxNoChangesBeforeClose <= 0 {
358			maxNoChangesBeforeClose = 3
359		}
360		heartbeatInterval := rcfg.HeartbeatInterval()
361		if heartbeatInterval <= 0 {
362			heartbeatInterval = bead.HeartbeatInterval
363		}
364		harness := rcfg.Harness()
365		model := rcfg.Model()
366		profile := rcfg.Profile()
367	
368		result := &ExecuteBeadLoopResult{}
369		attempted := make(map[string]struct{})
370		// triagedWarned suppresses the one-time boot warning when ComplexityGate is
371		// nil. One warning per Run() invocation is enough; the bool resets on the
372		// next worker boot.
373		triagedWarned := false
374		// hookFailed tracks beads whose pre-claim hook failed on first presentation
375		// in this run. A bead in hookFailed but not attempted gets one retry: on the
376		// second hook failure it moves to attempted so nextCandidate will skip it and
377		// the loop can exit gracefully. This prevents infinite spinning when the hook
378		// always fails while still allowing transient hook failures to be retried.
379		hookFailed := make(map[string]struct{})
380	
381		emit := func(eventType string, data map[string]any) {
382			writeLoopEvent(runtime.EventSink, runtime.SessionID, eventType, data, now().UTC())
383		}
384	
385		emit("loop.start", map[string]any{
386			"worker_id":    runtime.WorkerID,
387			"project_root": runtime.ProjectRoot,
388			"harness":      harness,
389			"model":        model,
390			"session_id":   runtime.SessionID,
391			"assignee":     assignee,
392			"once":         runtime.Once,
393		})
394		defer func() {
395			emit("loop.end", map[string]any{
396				"attempts":            result.Attempts,
397				"successes":           result.Successes,
398				"failures":            result.Failures,
399				"last_failure_status": result.LastFailureStatus,
400			})
401		}()
402	
403		for {
404			// Respect context cancellation between iterations. Without this check,
405			// a Stop() request (which cancels ctx) would only take effect during
406			// the idle poll sleep — the loop would happily claim the next ready
407			// bead as soon as the current Execute returned, ignoring the cancel.
408			if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
409				return result, err
410			}
411	
412			candidate, ok, err := w.nextCandidate(attempted, runtime.LabelFilter)
413			if err != nil {
414				return result, err
415			}
416			if !ok {
417				if result.Attempts == 0 {
418					result.NoReadyWork = true
419					if diag, ok := w.Store.(readyDiagnoser); ok {
420						if breakdown, bErr := diag.ReadyExecutionBreakdown(); bErr == nil {
421							result.NoReadyWorkDetail = NoReadyWorkBreakdown{
422								SkippedEpics:       breakdown.SkippedEpics,
423								SkippedOnCooldown:  breakdown.SkippedOnCooldown,
424								SkippedNotEligible: breakdown.SkippedNotEligible,
425								SkippedSuperseded:  breakdown.SkippedSuperseded,
426								NextRetryAfter:     breakdown.NextRetryAfter,
427							}
428						}
429					}
430				}
431				if runtime.PollInterval <= 0 {
432					return result, nil
433				}
434				if err := sleepWithContext(ctx, runtime.PollInterval); err != nil {
435					return result, err
436				}
437				continue
438			}
439	
440			// Pre-claim hook: fetch origin + verify ancestry before claiming.
441			// On error the bead is skipped for this iteration; the loop
442			// continues (ctx is not cancelled). The bead is NOT added to
443			// attempted on the FIRST failure so a transient hook failure (e.g.
444			// diverged branch) allows a retry on the next iteration. On a
445			// SECOND failure for the same bead in the same run it is moved into
446			// attempted so nextCandidate will skip it, preventing an infinite
447			// loop when the hook always fails.
448			if runtime.PreClaimHook != nil {
449				if hookErr := runtime.PreClaimHook(ctx); hookErr != nil {
450					if runtime.Log != nil {
451						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "pre-claim hook: %v (skipping %s)\n", hookErr, candidate.ID)
452					}
453					emit("preclaim.skipped", map[string]any{
454						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
455						"reason":  hookErr.Error(),
456					})
457					if _, alreadyFailed := hookFailed[candidate.ID]; alreadyFailed {
458						// Second failure in this run: stop retrying this bead.
459						attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}
460					} else {
461						hookFailed[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}
462					}
463					continue
464				}
465			}
466	
467			attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}
468	
469			// Routing preflight gate (FEAT-006 D3, ddx-98e6e9ef): consult the
470			// upstream typed-incompatibility surface BEFORE claiming. If the
471			// configured (harness, model) cannot serve the bead, exit the loop
472			// with a worker-level failure record — no claim, no executor
473			// invocation, no tier-attempt event burn.
474			if runtime.RoutePreflight != nil {
475				if rerr := runtime.RoutePreflight(ctx, harness, model); rerr != nil {
476					detail := fmt.Sprintf("routing preflight rejected (harness=%s model=%s): %s",
477						harness, model, rerr.Error())
478					if runtime.Log != nil {
479						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "routing preflight: %s (skipping %s, exiting loop)\n",
480							detail, candidate.ID)
481					}
482					emit("preflight.rejected", map[string]any{
483						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
484						"harness": harness,
485						"model":   model,
486						"reason":  rerr.Error(),
487					})
488					report := ExecuteBeadReport{
489						BeadID:  candidate.ID,
490						Status:  ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
491						Detail:  detail,
492						Harness: harness,
493						Model:   model,
494					}
495					result.Attempts++
496					result.Failures++
497					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
498					result.Results = append(result.Results, report)
499					return result, nil
500				}
501			}
502	
503			// Complexity triage gate (ddx-5bf4ee7e): pre-Claim decomposition check.
504			// When the gate is nil, emit a one-time boot warning — the gate must not
505			// be silently optional.
506			if w.ComplexityGate == nil {
507				if !triagedWarned {
508					if runtime.Log != nil {
509						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log,
510							"warning: triage complexity gate is disabled; coarse beads may waste dispatch attempts\n")
511					}
512					emit("triage.gate_disabled", map[string]any{"bead_id": candidate.ID})
513					triagedWarned = true
514				}
515			} else {
516				shouldClaim, triageErr := w.ComplexityGate(ctx, candidate)
517				if triageErr != nil {
518					if runtime.Log != nil {
519						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log,
520							"triage gate error for %s: %v (skipping)\n", candidate.ID, triageErr)
521					}
522					emit("triage.error", map[string]any{
523						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
524						"reason":  triageErr.Error(),
525					})
526					// A gate error does not count as an attempt; re-pick next candidate.
527					continue
528				}
529				if !shouldClaim {
530					if runtime.Log != nil {
531						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log,
532							"triage: skipped dispatch of %s (classified as non-atomic)\n", candidate.ID)
533					}
534					emit("triage.skipped", map[string]any{"bead_id": candidate.ID})
535					continue
536				}
537			}
538	
539			if err := w.Store.Claim(candidate.ID, assignee); err != nil {
540				continue
541			}
542	
543			emit("bead.claimed", map[string]any{
544				"bead_id":  candidate.ID,
545				"title":    candidate.Title,
546				"assignee": assignee,
547			})
548	
549			if runtime.Log != nil {
550				if candidate.Title != "" {
551					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "\n▶ %s: %s\n", candidate.ID, candidate.Title)
552				} else {
553					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "\n▶ %s\n", candidate.ID)
554				}
555			}
556	
557			// Generate a provisional attempt_id for progress events.
558			// The real attempt_id is assigned inside ExecuteBead; we use this
559			// for queueing/running events and replace with the real one once known.
560			provAttemptID := time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405") + "-" + randomProgressID()
561			runStart := now()
562			phaseSeq := 0
563			nextPhase := func(phase string, heartbeat bool) {
564				phaseSeq++
565				emitProgress(runtime.ProgressCh, newProgressEvent(
566					runtime.WorkerID, runtime.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, provAttemptID,
567					harness, model, profile,
568					phase, phaseSeq, heartbeat, now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
569				))
570			}
571	
572			nextPhase("queueing", false)
573	
574			hbCtx, hbCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
575			var hbWG sync.WaitGroup
576			hbWG.Add(1)
577			go func(beadID string) {
578				defer hbWG.Done()
579				ticker := time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval)
580				defer ticker.Stop()
581				for {
582					select {
583					case <-hbCtx.Done():
584						return
585					case <-ticker.C:
586						_ = w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID)
587					}
588				}
589			}(candidate.ID)
590	
591			nextPhase("running", false)
592	
593			report, err := w.Executor.Execute(ctx, candidate.ID)
594			hbCancel()
595			hbWG.Wait()
596			if err != nil {
597				report = ExecuteBeadReport{
598					BeadID: candidate.ID,
599					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
600					Detail: err.Error(),
601				}
602			}
603			if report.BeadID == "" {
604				report.BeadID = candidate.ID
605			}
606			if report.Status == "" {
607				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
608			}
609			if report.Detail == "" {
610				report.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(report.Status, "", "")
611			}
612	
613			result.Attempts++
614	
615			if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess {
616				appendLoopRoutingEvidence(w.Store, candidate.ID, report, now().UTC())
617				// Close the bead early when review is skipped. The closure gate
618				// (ddx-e30e60a9) accepts this path because closing_commit_sha is
619				// set and there is no malformed-APPROVE event to reject.
620				reviewApproved := true
621				reviewSkipped := w.Reviewer == nil || runtime.NoReview || HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, "review:skip")
622	
623				if reviewSkipped {
624					if err := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); err != nil {
625						if w.handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx, candidate.ID, "CloseWithEvidence", err, assignee, result, runtime, now) {
626							continue
627						}
628						return result, ctx.Err()
629					}
630				}
631	
632				if w.Reviewer != nil && !runtime.NoReview && !HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, "review:skip") {
633					reviewRes, reviewErr := w.Reviewer.ReviewBead(ctx, candidate.ID, report.ResultRev, report.Harness, report.Model)
634					if reviewErr != nil {
635						// FEAT-022 §12+§14: classify the failure into the four-class
636						// taxonomy and count prior review-error events scoped to the
637						// current result_rev. On the Nth failure emit a terminal
638						// review-manual-required event instead of yet another
639						// review-error, parking the bead so a subsequent loop
640						// iteration does NOT re-execute primary.
641						class := classifyReviewError(reviewErr, reviewRes)
642						prior := countPriorReviewErrors(w.Store, candidate.ID, report.ResultRev)
643						attemptCount := prior + 1
644						maxRetries := rcfg.ReviewMaxRetries()
645						if maxRetries <= 0 {
646							maxRetries = DefaultReviewMaxRetries
647						}
648						errSummary := EventBodySummary{
649							InputBytes:  0,
650							OutputBytes: 0,
651						}
652						if reviewRes != nil {
653							errSummary = EventBodySummary{
654								Harness:     reviewRes.ReviewerHarness,
655								Model:       reviewRes.ReviewerModel,
656								InputBytes:  reviewRes.InputBytes,
657								OutputBytes: reviewRes.OutputBytes,
658								ElapsedMS:   reviewRes.DurationMS,
659							}
660						}
661						if attemptCount >= maxRetries {
662							body := appendEventSummary(reviewErrorEventBody(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewErr.Error()), errSummary)
663							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
664								Kind:      "review-manual-required",
665								Summary:   class,
666								Body:      body,
667								Actor:     assignee,
668								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
669								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
670							})
671							// Park the bead with a long cooldown so subsequent
672							// loop iterations do NOT re-pick it for primary
673							// re-execution. Reviewer-failure invariant (§13) is
674							// preserved: the bead is NOT closed.
675							parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(capLoopCooldown(MaxLoopCooldown))
676							_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, parkUntil, "review-manual-required", class)
677						} else {
678							body := appendEventSummary(reviewErrorEventBody(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewErr.Error()), errSummary)
679							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
680								Kind:      "review-error",
681								Summary:   class,
682								Body:      body,
683								Actor:     assignee,
684								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
685								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
686							})
687						}
688					} else {
689						report.ReviewVerdict = string(reviewRes.Verdict)
690						report.ReviewRationale = reviewRes.Rationale
691						// Persist the full reviewer stream as an artifact so the
692						// event body never carries the raw stream (ddx-f8a11202).
693						// On error the artifact path is empty and the event body
694						// still contains the short verdict summary; callers of this
695						// loop can recover the full text from the reviewer session
696						// log if the artifact write failed.
697						artifactPath, artifactErr := persistReviewerStream(runtime.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, report.AttemptID, reviewRes.RawOutput)
698						if artifactErr != nil && runtime.Log != nil {
699							_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "reviewer stream artifact: %v\n", artifactErr)
700						}
701	
702						reviewSummary := EventBodySummary{
703							Harness:     reviewRes.ReviewerHarness,
704							Model:       reviewRes.ReviewerModel,
705							InputBytes:  reviewRes.InputBytes,
706							OutputBytes: reviewRes.OutputBytes,
707							ElapsedMS:   reviewRes.DurationMS,
708						}
709						switch reviewRes.Verdict {
710						case VerdictApprove:
711							// Approved: record the verdict event and then close.
712							// Closure must land AFTER the review event so the
713							// gate (ddx-e30e60a9) sees the terminal verdict.
714							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
715								Kind:      "review",
716								Summary:   "APPROVE",
717								Body:      appendEventSummary(reviewEventBody("APPROVE", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath), reviewSummary),
718								Actor:     assignee,
719								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
720								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
721							})
722							if cerr := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); cerr != nil {
723								if w.handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx, candidate.ID, "CloseWithEvidence", cerr, assignee, result, runtime, now) {
724									continue
725								}
726								return result, ctx.Err()
727							}
728						case VerdictRequestChanges:
729							// Needs fixes: record the review verdict, then reopen
730							// with findings in notes. The review event must land
731							// even on non-approve paths so review-outcomes can
732							// attribute the rejection to the originating tier.
733							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
734								Kind:      "review",
735								Summary:   "REQUEST_CHANGES",
736								Body:      appendEventSummary(reviewEventBody("REQUEST_CHANGES", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath), reviewSummary),
737								Actor:     assignee,
738								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
739								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
740							})
741							reopenNotes := reviewRes.Rationale
742							if reopenNotes == "" {
743								reopenNotes = reviewRes.RawOutput
744							}
745							if reopenErr := w.Store.Reopen(candidate.ID, "review: REQUEST_CHANGES", reopenNotes); reopenErr != nil {
746								if w.handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx, candidate.ID, "Reopen", reopenErr, assignee, result, runtime, now) {
747									continue
748								}
749								return result, ctx.Err()
750							}
751							report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges
752							report.Detail = "post-merge review: REQUEST_CHANGES"
753							reviewApproved = false
754						case VerdictBlock:
755							rationale := strings.TrimSpace(reviewRes.Rationale)
756							if rationale == "" {
757								_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
758									Kind:      "review-malfunction",
759									Summary:   "BLOCK without rationale",
760									Body:      appendEventSummary(reviewEventBody("BLOCK without rationale", "", artifactPath), reviewSummary),
761									Actor:     assignee,
762									Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
763									CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
764								})
765								report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
766								report.Detail = "post-merge review: malformed BLOCK verdict (missing rationale)"
767								report.ReviewRationale = ""
768								reviewApproved = false
769								break
770							}
771							// Cannot proceed: record the verdict, then reopen and
772							// flag for human with BLOCK marker plus actionable rationale.
773							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
774								Kind:      "review",
775								Summary:   "BLOCK",
776								Body:      appendEventSummary(rationale, reviewSummary),
777								Actor:     assignee,
778								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
779								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
780							})
781							blockNotes := "REVIEW:BLOCK\n\n" + rationale
782							if reopenErr := w.Store.Reopen(candidate.ID, "review: BLOCK", blockNotes); reopenErr != nil {
783								if w.handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx, candidate.ID, "Reopen", reopenErr, assignee, result, runtime, now) {
784									continue
785								}
786								return result, ctx.Err()
787							}
788							report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock
789							report.Detail = "post-merge review: BLOCK (flagged for human)"
790							reviewApproved = false
791						}
792					}
793				}
794	
795				if reviewApproved {
796					result.Successes++
797					result.LastSuccessAt = now().UTC()
798				} else {
799					result.Failures++
800					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
801				}
802			} else if shouldAttemptConflictRecovery(report, runtime) {
803				// Attempt to reuse the preserved iteration before discarding the
804				// agent's work. On success the bead closes as a normal merge; on
805				// failure the bead parks under LandConflictCooldown with a
806				// structured outcome (land_conflict_unresolvable or
807				// land_conflict_needs_human).
808				report = w.runConflictRecovery(ctx, candidate, report, runtime, assignee, now)
809				if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess {
810					if err := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); err != nil {
811						if w.handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx, candidate.ID, "CloseWithEvidence", err, assignee, result, runtime, now) {
812							continue
813						}
814						return result, ctx.Err()
815					}
816					result.Successes++
817					result.LastSuccessAt = now().UTC()
818				} else {
819					if err := w.Store.Unclaim(candidate.ID); err != nil {
820						if w.handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx, candidate.ID, "Unclaim", err, assignee, result, runtime, now) {
821							continue
822						}
823						return result, ctx.Err()
824					}
825					parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(LandConflictCooldown)
826					if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, parkUntil, report.Status, report.Detail); err != nil {
827						if w.handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx, candidate.ID, "SetExecutionCooldown", err, assignee, result, runtime, now) {
828							continue
829						}
830						return result, ctx.Err()
831					}
832					report.RetryAfter = parkUntil.Format(time.RFC3339)
833					result.Failures++
834					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
835				}
836			} else {
837				if err := w.Store.Unclaim(candidate.ID); err != nil {
838					if w.handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx, candidate.ID, "Unclaim", err, assignee, result, runtime, now) {
839						continue
840					}
841					return result, ctx.Err()
842				}
843				if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview {
844					if err := w.Store.AppendNotes(candidate.ID, preservedNeedsReviewNote(report)); err != nil {
845						if w.handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx, candidate.ID, "AppendNotes", err, assignee, result, runtime, now) {
846							continue
847						}
848						return result, ctx.Err()
849					}
850				}
851				if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges {
852					count, cerr := w.Store.IncrNoChangesCount(candidate.ID)
853					if cerr != nil {
854						if w.handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx, candidate.ID, "IncrNoChangesCount", cerr, assignee, result, runtime, now) {
855							continue
856						}
857						return result, ctx.Err()
858					}
859					satisfied, evidence, aerr := w.adjudicateNoChanges(ctx, candidate.ID, count, maxNoChangesBeforeClose, report.NoChangesRationale, candidate.Acceptance, runtime.ProjectRoot)
860					if aerr != nil {
861						if w.handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx, candidate.ID, "adjudicateNoChanges", aerr, assignee, result, runtime, now) {
862							continue
863						}
864						return result, ctx.Err()
865					}
866					if satisfied {
867						// Adjudication confirmed bead is already satisfied.
868						// Set the terminal status BEFORE the close so the late
869						// executeBeadLoopEvent append captures "already_satisfied"
870						// (not "no_changes"), and emit an early execute-bead
871						// evidence event so the closure gate accepts even when
872						// BaseRev is empty (test fixtures and genuinely-no-commit
873						// satisfied beads).
874						report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied
875						if evidence != "" {
876							// Checker evidence explains why the bead is being closed;
877							// it takes precedence over the executor's attempt detail.
878							report.Detail = evidence
879						}
880						_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, executeBeadLoopEvent(report, assignee, now().UTC()))
881						if cerr := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.BaseRev); cerr != nil {
882							if w.handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx, candidate.ID, "CloseWithEvidence", cerr, assignee, result, runtime, now) {
883								continue
884							}
885							return result, ctx.Err()
886						}
887						result.Successes++
888						result.LastSuccessAt = now().UTC()
889					} else {
890						// Unresolved: suppress immediate retry so the queue can
891						// move on to other beads.
892						if shouldSuppressNoProgress(report) {
893							retryAfter := now().UTC().Add(capLoopCooldown(noProgressCooldown))
894							if cerr := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, retryAfter, report.Status, report.Detail); cerr != nil {
895								if w.handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx, candidate.ID, "SetExecutionCooldown", cerr, assignee, result, runtime, now) {
896									continue
897								}
898								return result, ctx.Err()
899							}
900							report.RetryAfter = retryAfter.Format(time.RFC3339)
901						}
902						result.Failures++
903						result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
904					}
905				} else if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition {
906					// Executor declined the bead because it is too large to
907					// deliver in one pass. Record a structured
908					// `decomposition-recommendation` event and park the bead
909					// long enough that subsequent loop iterations do NOT
910					// re-attempt it. An operator (or helix-evolve) clears the
911					// cooldown — typically by splitting the bead.
912					body, mErr := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
913						"rationale":            report.DecompositionRationale,
914						"recommended_subbeads": report.DecompositionRecommendation,
915						"detail":               report.Detail,
916						"base_rev":             report.BaseRev,
917						"session_id":           report.SessionID,
918					})
919					bodyStr := ""
920					if mErr == nil {
921						bodyStr = string(body)
922					} else {
923						bodyStr = fmt.Sprintf("rationale=%s\nrecommended_subbeads=%v",
924							report.DecompositionRationale, report.DecompositionRecommendation)
925					}
926					summary := "agent declined: needs decomposition"
927					if n := len(report.DecompositionRecommendation); n > 0 {
928						summary = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%d sub-beads)", summary, n)
929					}
930					_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
931						Kind:      "decomposition-recommendation",
932						Summary:   summary,
933						Body:      bodyStr,
934						Actor:     assignee,
935						Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
936						CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
937					})
938					parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(capLoopCooldown(MaxLoopCooldown))
939					if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, parkUntil, report.Status, report.Detail); err != nil {
940						if w.handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx, candidate.ID, "SetExecutionCooldown", err, assignee, result, runtime, now) {
941							continue
942						}
943						return result, ctx.Err()
944					}
945					report.RetryAfter = parkUntil.Format(time.RFC3339)
946					result.Failures++
947					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
948				} else if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed {
949					// Push failed after a successful local merge/ff. The bead is
950					// NOT closed (commits live only locally) — park it under the
951					// 24h cap so the loop re-attempts within a reasonable horizon.
952					// The push stderr in report.Detail is recorded as last_detail
953					// so it surfaces on the bead and on any direct claim attempt.
954					parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(capLoopCooldown(MaxLoopCooldown))
955					if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, parkUntil, report.Status, report.Detail); err != nil {
956						if w.handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx, candidate.ID, "SetExecutionCooldown", err, assignee, result, runtime, now) {
957							continue
958						}
959						return result, ctx.Err()
960					}
961					report.RetryAfter = parkUntil.Format(time.RFC3339)
962					result.Failures++
963					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
964				} else if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict {
965					// The local target ref was advanced and the loop's
966					// auto-pull/merge/retry recovery hit a merge conflict it
967					// cannot resolve without operator input. Record a
968					// `push-conflict` event with the conflict context so the
969					// operator can find and resolve it, and park the bead under
970					// the same 24h cap as push_failed.
971					body, mErr := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
972						"detail":     report.Detail,
973						"base_rev":   report.BaseRev,
974						"result_rev": report.ResultRev,
975						"session_id": report.SessionID,
976					})
977					bodyStr := report.Detail
978					if mErr == nil {
979						bodyStr = string(body)
980					}
981					_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
982						Kind:      "push-conflict",
983						Summary:   "auto-merge after push race could not be resolved",
984						Body:      bodyStr,
985						Actor:     assignee,
986						Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
987						CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
988					})
989					parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(capLoopCooldown(MaxLoopCooldown))
990					if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, parkUntil, report.Status, report.Detail); err != nil {
991						if w.handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx, candidate.ID, "SetExecutionCooldown", err, assignee, result, runtime, now) {
992							continue
993						}
994						return result, ctx.Err()
995					}
996					report.RetryAfter = parkUntil.Format(time.RFC3339)
997					result.Failures++
998					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
999				} else {
1000					if shouldSuppressNoProgress(report) {
1001						retryAfter := now().UTC().Add(capLoopCooldown(noProgressCooldown))
1002						if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, retryAfter, report.Status, report.Detail); err != nil {
1003							if w.handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx, candidate.ID, "SetExecutionCooldown", err, assignee, result, runtime, now) {
1004								continue
1005							}
1006							return result, ctx.Err()
1007						}
1008						report.RetryAfter = retryAfter.Format(time.RFC3339)
1009					}
1010					result.Failures++
1011					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
1012				}
1013			}
1014	
1015			result.Results = append(result.Results, report)
1016	
1017			// Skip the late execute-bead append for already-satisfied beads —
1018			// the satisfied path appends its own terminal event before
1019			// CloseWithEvidence so the closure gate sees execution evidence.
1020			// Duplicating it here would yield two identical events.
1021			if report.Status != ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied {
1022				if err := w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, executeBeadLoopEvent(report, assignee, now().UTC())); err != nil {
1023					// Event recording failure is non-terminal: log it and continue.
1024					// result counters were already updated by the outcome block above;
1025					// do not double-count by calling handleOutcomeStoreError.
1026					if runtime.Log != nil {
1027						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "outcome store error (AppendEvent %s): %v (continuing)\n", candidate.ID, err)
1028					}
1029					if ctx.Err() == nil {
1030						_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, now().UTC().Add(StoreErrorCooldown), "loop-error", "AppendEvent: "+err.Error())
1031					}
1032					if ctx.Err() != nil {
1033						return result, ctx.Err()
1034					}
1035					continue
1036				}
1037			}
1038	
1039			// Emit terminal progress phase event.
1040			terminalPhase := "failed"
1041			if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess || report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied {
1042				terminalPhase = "done"
1043			} else if report.PreserveRef != "" {
1044				terminalPhase = "preserved"
1045			}
1046			// Use the real attempt_id from the report if available.
1047			finalAttemptID := report.AttemptID
1048			if finalAttemptID == "" {
1049				finalAttemptID = provAttemptID
1050			}
1051			phaseSeq++
1052			emitProgress(runtime.ProgressCh, ProgressEvent{
1053				EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
1054				AttemptID: finalAttemptID,
1055				WorkerID:  runtime.WorkerID,
1056				ProjectID: runtime.ProjectRoot,
1057				BeadID:    candidate.ID,
1058				Harness:   harness,
1059				Model:     model,
1060				Profile:   profile,
1061				Phase:     terminalPhase,
1062				PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
1063				Heartbeat: false,
1064				TS:        now().UTC(),
1065				ElapsedMS: now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
1066				Message:   report.Detail,
1067			})
1068	
1069			emit("bead.result", map[string]any{
1070				"bead_id":              candidate.ID,
1071				"status":               report.Status,
1072				"detail":               report.Detail,
1073				"session_id":           report.SessionID,
1074				"result_rev":           report.ResultRev,
1075				"base_rev":             report.BaseRev,
1076				"preserve_ref":         report.PreserveRef,
1077				"no_changes_rationale": report.NoChangesRationale,
1078				"duration_ms":          now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
1079			})
1080	
1081			if runtime.Log != nil {
1082				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "✓ %s → %s\n", candidate.ID, formatLoopResult(report))
1083			}
1084	
1085			if runtime.Once {
1086				return result, nil
1087			}
1088		}
1089	}

1091	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) nextCandidate(attempted map[string]struct{}, labelFilter string) (bead.Bead, bool, error) {
1092		ready, err := w.Store.ReadyExecution()
1093		if err != nil {
1094			return bead.Bead{}, false, err
1095		}
1096		for _, candidate := range ready {
1097			if _, seen := attempted[candidate.ID]; seen {
1098				continue
1099			}
1100			if labelFilter != "" && !HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, labelFilter) {
1101				continue
1102			}
1103			return candidate, true, nil
1104		}
1105		return bead.Bead{}, false, nil
1106	}

1108	// appendLoopRoutingEvidence records a kind:routing evidence event on the bead
1109	// from the executor's ExecuteBeadReport, so that review-outcomes analytics can
1110	// attribute a subsequent review verdict to the originating provider/model tier.
1111	// Best-effort: errors and missing-provider cases are silently ignored.
1112	func appendLoopRoutingEvidence(store BeadEventAppender, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport, createdAt time.Time) {
1113		if store == nil || beadID == "" {
1114			return
1115		}
1116		provider := report.Provider
1117		if provider == "" {
1118			provider = report.Harness
1119		}
1120		if provider == "" {
1121			return
1122		}
1123		body, err := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
1124			"resolved_provider": provider,
1125			"resolved_model":    report.Model,
1126			"fallback_chain":    []string{},
1127			"requested_profile": report.RequestedProfile,
1128			"requested_tier":    report.RequestedTier,
1129			"resolved_tier":     report.ResolvedTier,
1130			"escalation_count":  report.EscalationCount,
1131			"final_tier":        report.FinalTier,
1132		})
1133		if err != nil {
1134			return
1135		}
1136		summary := "provider=" + provider
1137		if report.Model != "" {
1138			summary += " model=" + report.Model
1139		}
1140		_ = store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
1141			Kind:      "routing",
1142			Summary:   summary,
1143			Body:      string(body),
1144			Actor:     "ddx",
1145			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
1146			CreatedAt: createdAt,
1147		})
1148	}

1150	func executeBeadLoopEvent(report ExecuteBeadReport, actor string, createdAt time.Time) bead.BeadEvent {
1151		parts := []string{}
1152		if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview {
1153			parts = append(parts, preservedNeedsReviewNote(report))
1154		}
1155		if report.Detail != "" {
1156			parts = append(parts, report.Detail)
1157		}
1158		if report.Tier != "" {
1159			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("tier=%s", report.Tier))
1160		}
1161		if report.ProbeResult != "" {
1162			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("probe_result=%s", report.ProbeResult))
1163		}
1164		if report.NoChangesRationale != "" {
1165			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("rationale: %s", report.NoChangesRationale))
1166		}
1167		if report.ReviewRationale != "" {
1168			parts = append(parts, report.ReviewRationale)
1169		}
1170		if report.PreserveRef != "" {
1171			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("preserve_ref=%s", report.PreserveRef))
1172		}
1173		if report.ResultRev != "" {
1174			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("result_rev=%s", report.ResultRev))
1175		}
1176		if report.BaseRev != "" {
1177			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("base_rev=%s", report.BaseRev))
1178		}
1179		if report.RetryAfter != "" {
1180			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("retry_after=%s", report.RetryAfter))
1181		}
1182	
1183		return bead.BeadEvent{
1184			Kind:      "execute-bead",
1185			Summary:   report.Status,
1186			Body:      strings.Join(parts, "\n"),
1187			Actor:     actor,
1188			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
1189			CreatedAt: createdAt,
1190		}
1191	}

1193	func preservedNeedsReviewNote(report ExecuteBeadReport) string {
1194		parts := []string{
1195			"preserved-needs-review",
1196			"gate_summary=" + oneLineGateSummary(report.Detail),
1197		}
1198		if report.PreserveRef != "" {
1199			parts = append(parts, "preserve_ref="+report.PreserveRef)
1200		}
1201		if report.ResultRev != "" {
1202			parts = append(parts, "result_rev="+report.ResultRev)
1203		}
1204		return strings.Join(parts, "\n")
1205	}

1207	func oneLineGateSummary(detail string) string {
1208		detail = strings.TrimSpace(detail)
1209		if detail == "" {
1210			return "safety gate preserved result"
1211		}
1212		if idx := strings.IndexByte(detail, '\n'); idx >= 0 {
1213			detail = strings.TrimSpace(detail[:idx])
1214		}
1215		return detail
1216	}

1218	// writeLoopEvent emits one structured JSONL line to sink describing a
1219	// milestone in an execute-bead loop run. Entries use the same envelope as
1220	// the ddx-agent harness (session_id/seq/type/ts/data) so existing log
1221	// aggregators (FormatSessionLogLines, ddx server workers log) can parse
1222	// the stream uniformly. Errors are swallowed: structured logging must
1223	// never break the core execute-loop.
1224	func writeLoopEvent(sink io.Writer, sessionID, eventType string, data map[string]any, ts time.Time) {
1225		if sink == nil {
1226			return
1227		}
1228		if data == nil {
1229			data = map[string]any{}
1230		}
1231		entry := map[string]any{
1232			"session_id": sessionID,
1233			"type":       eventType,
1234			"ts":         ts.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
1235			"data":       data,
1236		}
1237		line, err := json.Marshal(entry)
1238		if err != nil {
1239			return
1240		}
1241		_, _ = sink.Write(line)
1242		_, _ = sink.Write([]byte("\n"))
1243	}

1245	// formatLoopResult returns a concise human-readable summary of a bead execution
1246	// result using merged/preserved/error terminology instead of raw status codes.
1247	func formatLoopResult(report ExecuteBeadReport) string {
1248		switch report.Status {
1249		case ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess:
1250			shortRev := report.ResultRev
1251			if len(shortRev) > 8 {
1252				shortRev = shortRev[:8]
1253			}
1254			if shortRev != "" {
1255				return fmt.Sprintf("merged (%s)", shortRev)
1256			}
1257			return "merged"
1258		case ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
1259			return "already_satisfied"

1261			if report.NoChangesRationale != "" {
1262				return fmt.Sprintf("no_changes: %s", report.NoChangesRationale)
1263			}
1264			return "no_changes"
1265		default:
1266			detail := report.Detail
1267			if detail == "" {
1268				detail = report.Status
1269			}
1270			if report.PreserveRef != "" {
1271				return fmt.Sprintf("preserved: %s", detail)
1272			}

1277	// reCommitSHA matches a 7-to-40 character lowercase hex string that looks like a
1278	// git commit SHA. Used to detect whether a no_changes rationale cites a prior commit.
1279	var reCommitSHA = regexp.MustCompile(`\b[0-9a-f]{7,40}\b`)
1280	
1281	// reTestFuncName matches a Go test function name (TestXxx or BenchmarkXxx).
1282	var reTestFuncName = regexp.MustCompile(`\b(?:Test|Benchmark)[A-Z]\w*\b`)
1283	
1284	// rationaleIsSpecific returns true when the rationale string contains a reference
1285	// specific enough to treat a no_changes outcome as already_satisfied on the first
1286	// attempt. Currently this means: the rationale cites a commit SHA (7+ hex chars)
1287	// or a Go test function name. Vague rationales ("nothing to do") return false.
1288	func rationaleIsSpecific(rationale string) bool {
1289		if rationale == "" {
1290			return false
1291		}
1292		return reCommitSHA.MatchString(rationale) || reTestFuncName.MatchString(rationale)
1293	}
1294	
1295	// adjudicateNoChanges runs the no-change adjudication step for a bead.
1296	// It returns (satisfied, evidence, err). When satisfied is true the bead
1297	// should be closed as already_satisfied with the evidence string. When false
1298	// retry suppression (cooldown) should be applied and the bead left open.
1299	//
1300	// If a SatisfactionChecker is configured it is called first. Otherwise:
1301	//   - When the report carries a specific rationale (cites a commit SHA or test
1302	//     name), the bead is closed as already_satisfied on the first occurrence.
1303	//   - Otherwise the default count-based rule applies (close after maxNoChangesBeforeClose).
1304	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) adjudicateNoChanges(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount, maxNoChangesBeforeClose int, rationale, acceptance, projectRoot string) (bool, string, error) {
1305		if w.SatisfactionChecker != nil {
1306			return w.SatisfactionChecker.CheckSatisfied(ctx, beadID, noChangesCount)
1307		}
1308		candidate := false
1309		evidence := ""
1310		switch {
1311		case rationaleIsSpecific(rationale):
1312			candidate = true
1313			evidence = rationale
1314		case noChangesCount >= maxNoChangesBeforeClose:
1315			candidate = true
1316			evidence = fmt.Sprintf("no_changes on %d consecutive attempt(s); bead treated as already satisfied", noChangesCount)
1317		}
1318		if !candidate {
1319			return false, "", nil
1320		}
1321		// Tighten the gate: when AC names structural properties (test functions,
1322		// deleted files, removed struct fields), refuse already_satisfied unless
1323		// each property holds in the worktree / rationale. Prevents false closes
1324		// where a regression suite passes but the AC's specific contract is unmet.
1325		if claims := ParseACClaims(acceptance); len(claims) > 0 {
1326			if ok, why := VerifyACClaims(claims, projectRoot, rationale); !ok {
1327				return false, why, nil
1328			}
1329		}
1330		return true, evidence, nil
1331	}

1333	func shouldSuppressNoProgress(report ExecuteBeadReport) bool {
1334		if report.BaseRev == "" || report.ResultRev == "" {
1335			return false
1336		}
1337		return report.BaseRev == report.ResultRev
1338	}

1340	// classifyReviewError maps a reviewer error and partial result into one of the
1341	// four FEAT-022 §12 taxonomy classes. Resolution order:
1342	//  1. reviewRes.Error if it's already one of the canonical class identifiers
1343	//     (the reviewer set it explicitly — preferred path).
1344	//  2. The error string text (for backwards-compatible reviewers that only
1345	//     embed the class in their message).
1346	//  3. ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable as a fallback when the strict-parse error
1347	//     leaks through a reviewer that did not set Error.
1348	//  4. Default to transport (network/provider-side failure) when nothing else
1349	//     matches.
1350	func classifyReviewError(reviewErr error, reviewRes *ReviewResult) string {
1351		if reviewRes != nil {
1352			switch reviewRes.Error {
1353			case evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
1354				evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty,
1355				evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable,
1356				evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport:
1357				return reviewRes.Error
1358			}
1359		}
1360		msg := ""
1361		if reviewErr != nil {
1362			msg = reviewErr.Error()
1363		}
1364		switch {
1365		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow):
1366			return evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow
1367		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty):
1368			return evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty
1369		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable):
1370			return evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
1371		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport):
1372			return evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport
1373		case reviewErr != nil && strings.Contains(msg, ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable.Error()):
1374			return evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
1375		}
1376		return evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport
1377	}
1378	
1379	// reResultRevField extracts the result_rev value from a structured review-error
1380	// event body (one `result_rev=<sha>` line per event, written by
1381	// reviewErrorEventBody). The format is intentionally line-oriented so it
1382	// survives the AppendEvent body cap without losing the rev association.
1383	var reResultRevField = regexp.MustCompile(`(?m)^result_rev=([^\s]+)\s*$`)
1384	
1385	// countPriorReviewErrors returns the number of `review-error` events already
1386	// recorded against this bead whose body cites the given result_rev. This is
1387	// the FEAT-022 §14 retry counter — it is event-scoped (no separate counter
1388	// field on the bead) so a fresh result_rev naturally resets the count.
1389	func countPriorReviewErrors(store ExecuteBeadLoopStore, beadID, resultRev string) int {
1390		if resultRev == "" {
1391			return 0
1392		}
1393		events, err := store.Events(beadID)
1394		if err != nil {
1395			return 0
1396		}
1397		n := 0
1398		for _, ev := range events {
1399			if ev.Kind != "review-error" {
1400				continue
1401			}
1402			m := reResultRevField.FindStringSubmatch(ev.Body)
1403			if m == nil {
1404				continue
1405			}
1406			if m[1] == resultRev {
1407				n++
1408			}
1409		}
1410		return n
1411	}
1412	
1413	// reviewErrorEventBody is the canonical body shape for review-error and
1414	// review-manual-required events. It carries the failure class, attempt count,
1415	// and result_rev as discrete lines so operators can grep without parsing the
1416	// full reviewer error text. The trailing free-form message is the raw
1417	// reviewer-error string for forensics.
1418	func reviewErrorEventBody(class string, attemptCount int, resultRev, message string) string {
1419		var b strings.Builder
1420		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "failure_class=%s\n", class)
1421		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "attempt_count=%d\n", attemptCount)
1422		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "result_rev=%s\n", resultRev)
1423		if message != "" {
1424			b.WriteString("\n")
1425			b.WriteString(message)
1426		}
1427		return b.String()
1428	}
1429	
1430	// shouldAttemptConflictRecovery returns true when the loop should try to reuse
1431	// a preserved iteration ref before discarding it. Requires a non-empty
1432	// ProjectRoot so recovery git commands have a working directory, and a
1433	// non-empty PreserveRef to actually have something to recover.
1434	func shouldAttemptConflictRecovery(report ExecuteBeadReport, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) bool {
1435		if report.PreserveRef == "" || runtime.ProjectRoot == "" {
1436			return false
1437		}
1438		switch report.Status {
1439		case ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict:
1440			return true
1441		case ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed:
1442			// Attempt recovery when a commit was produced before the failure (e.g.
1443			// agent timed out mid-run). Both BaseRev and ResultRev must be set and
1444			// differ, because an empty BaseRev means we cannot confirm the agent
1445			// made any progress beyond the starting point.
1446			return report.BaseRev != "" && report.ResultRev != "" && report.ResultRev != report.BaseRev
1447		}
1448		return false
1449	}
1450	
1451	// runConflictRecovery attempts to recover a preserved iteration before the loop
1452	// parks the bead as failed. Step 1: 3-way ort auto-merge (-X ours). Step 2 (if
1453	// step 1 fails and ConflictResolver is set): focused conflict-resolve agent.
1454	// Returns an updated ExecuteBeadReport whose Status is success when recovery
1455	// landed the commit, or land_conflict_unresolvable/land_conflict_needs_human
1456	// when all recovery paths failed.
1457	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) runConflictRecovery(
1458		ctx context.Context,
1459		b bead.Bead,
1460		report ExecuteBeadReport,
1461		runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime,
1462		assignee string,
1463		now func() time.Time,
1464	) ExecuteBeadReport {
1465		var gitOps LandingGitOps = RealLandingGitOps{}
1466	
1467		autoFn := w.conflictAutoRecoverFn
1468		if autoFn == nil {
1469			autoFn = landConflictAutoRecover
1470		}
1471		newTip, autoErr := autoFn(runtime.ProjectRoot, report.PreserveRef, gitOps)
1472		if autoErr == nil && newTip != "" {
1473			_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(b.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
1474				Kind:      "land-conflict-auto-recovered",
1475				Summary:   "preserved iteration auto-recovered onto current tip via ort",
1476				Body:      fmt.Sprintf("preserve_ref=%s\nnew_tip=%s", report.PreserveRef, newTip),
1477				Actor:     assignee,
1478				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
1479				CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
1480			})
1481			report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
1482			report.ResultRev = newTip
1483			report.Detail = "auto-recovered: merged preserved iteration onto current tip via ort"
1484			return report
1485		}
1486	
1487		if w.ConflictResolver != nil {
1488			resolvedTip, isBlocking, resolveErr := w.ConflictResolver(ctx, b.ID, report.PreserveRef, runtime.ProjectRoot)
1489			if resolveErr == nil && resolvedTip != "" {
1490				_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(b.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
1491					Kind:      "land-conflict-auto-recovered",
1492					Summary:   "preserved iteration resolved by focused conflict-resolve agent",
1493					Body:      fmt.Sprintf("preserve_ref=%s\nnew_tip=%s", report.PreserveRef, resolvedTip),
1494					Actor:     assignee,
1495					Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
1496					CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
1497				})
1498				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
1499				report.ResultRev = resolvedTip
1500				report.Detail = "auto-recovered: focused conflict-resolve agent landed preserved iteration"
1501				return report
1502			}
1503			if isBlocking {
1504				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictNeedsHuman
1505			} else {
1506				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable
1507			}
1508		} else {
1509			report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable
1510		}
1511	
1512		autoErrMsg := ""
1513		if autoErr != nil {
1514			autoErrMsg = autoErr.Error()
1515		}
1516		body, mErr := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
1517			"preserve_ref":     report.PreserveRef,
1518			"base_rev":         report.BaseRev,
1519			"result_rev":       report.ResultRev,
1520			"session_id":       report.SessionID,
1521			"auto_merge_error": autoErrMsg,
1522		})
1523		bodyStr := report.PreserveRef
1524		if mErr == nil {
1525			bodyStr = string(body)
1526		}
1527		eventKind := "land-conflict-unresolvable"
1528		if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictNeedsHuman {
1529			eventKind = "land-conflict-needs-human"
1530		}
1531		_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(b.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
1532			Kind:      eventKind,
1533			Summary:   "preserved iteration could not be auto-recovered; parked for operator",
1534			Body:      bodyStr,
1535			Actor:     assignee,
1536			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
1537			CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
1538		})
1539		report.Detail = report.Status + ": preserve_ref=" + report.PreserveRef
1540		return report
1541	}

1543	func sleepWithContext(ctx context.Context, d time.Duration) error {
1544		if d <= 0 {
1545			return nil
1546		}
1547		timer := time.NewTimer(d)
1548		defer timer.Stop()
1549		select {
1550		case <-ctx.Done():
1551			return ctx.Err()
1552		case <-timer.C:
1553			return nil
1554		}
1555	}

Now let me walk through each AC item to confirm coverage before committing:

1. **AC1** - `EvaluateRetryPolicy` takes `actualPower` as a parameter (read from routing evidence); `ClassifyRetryOutcome` reads `status`+`failureMode` from attempt outcome. Tests read from evidence fields.

2. **AC2** - `TestEvaluateRetryPolicy_NoChangesEscalates` verifies NextMinPower > 0. `TestEvaluateRetryPolicy_MaxPowerPreserved` verifies MaxPower is never exceeded. `TestEvaluateRetryPolicy_MaxPowerNeverExceeded` verifies across several MaxPower values.

3. **AC3** - `computeNextMinPower` calls `TopNPowerThreshold(models, 1)`. `TestComputeNextMinPower_WithCatalog` verifies this.

4. **AC4** - `RetryDecision` struct has no Harness/Provider/Model/ModelRef fields — verified structurally in `TestEvaluateRetryPolicy_DecisionContainsNoPins`.

5. **AC5** - `EvaluateRetryPolicy` and `ClassifyRetryOutcome` accept only `failureMode` (a typed constant) from the passthrough context — they never inspect string values from Harness/Provider/Model. `TestClassifyRetryOutcome_PassthroughPriorityOverStatus` verifies passthrough failure modes work regardless of status strings.

6. **AC6** - Tests: `TestEvaluateRetryPolicy_SuccessNoRetry`, `TestEvaluateRetryPolicy_NoChangesEscalates`, `TestEvaluateRetryPolicy_ReviewBlockedCapabilityEscalates`, `TestEvaluateRetryPolicy_DecisionContainsNoPins`, `TestEvaluateRetryPolicy_MaxPowerPreserved`/`AlreadyAtTopTier`/`NoCatalogNoProgress`, `TestEvaluateRetryPolicy_SetupFailureDoesNotRetry`.

7. **AC7** - `TestEvaluateRetryPolicy_PassthroughExhaustedConstraint` and `TestEvaluateRetryPolicy_PassthroughExhaustedPower` verify both stop with no pin mutation. No `ResolveRoute` call appears in `retry_policy.go`.

8. **AC8** - `TestEvaluateRetryPolicy_AuditFieldsAlwaysPopulated` verifies Reason+Evidence populated across all outcome paths.

feat(agent): DDx-owned bead retry policy with power escalation [ddx-9f82f325]

Implement ClassifyRetryOutcome + EvaluateRetryPolicy so DDx can decide
whether to retry a failed bead by raising MinPower — without selecting or
pinning a concrete model, harness, provider, or model-ref.

- ClassifyRetryOutcome maps status+failure_mode to policy vocabulary:
  no_changes_after_attempt, review_blocked_capability,
  passthrough_exhausted (for both blocked_by_passthrough_constraint and
  agent_power_unsatisfied), deterministic_setup_failure, and others.
- EvaluateRetryPolicy returns a RetryDecision{ShouldRetry, NextMinPower,
  Reason, Evidence} with no harness/provider/model pins.
- computeNextMinPower calls TopNPowerThreshold(models, 1) to target the
  strongest tier; falls back to a heuristic bump when catalog is empty.
- MaxPower is always preserved: nextMinPower > maxPower → stop.
- Passthrough exhaustion always stops without inspecting string values.
- Deterministic setup failures (harness not installed, no viable provider,
  auth error) always stop; power escalation cannot fix them.
- Audit: every RetryDecision carries Reason + Evidence for event bodies.
- 22 tests cover all AC items including AC6 and AC7.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

Implement ClassifyRetryOutcome + EvaluateRetryPolicy so DDx can decide
whether to retry a failed bead by raising MinPower — without selecting or
pinning a concrete model, harness, provider, or model-ref.

- ClassifyRetryOutcome maps status+failure_mode to policy vocabulary:
  no_changes_after_attempt, review_blocked_capability,
  passthrough_exhausted (for blocked_by_passthrough_constraint and
  agent_power_unsatisfied), deterministic_setup_failure, and others.
- EvaluateRetryPolicy returns a RetryDecision{ShouldRetry, NextMinPower,
  Reason, Evidence} with no harness/provider/model pins.
- computeNextMinPower calls TopNPowerThreshold(models, 1) to target the
  strongest tier; falls back to a heuristic bump when catalog is empty.
- MaxPower is always preserved: nextMinPower > maxPower → stop.
- Passthrough exhaustion always stops without inspecting string values.
- Deterministic setup failures (harness not installed, no viable provider,
  auth error) always stop; power escalation cannot fix them.
- Audit: every RetryDecision carries Reason + Evidence for event bodies.
- 22 tests cover all AC items including AC6 and AC7.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1

The commit landed. The bead is complete. Here's the AC verification summary:

| AC | Evidence |
|---|---|
| AC1 | `EvaluateRetryPolicy(outcome, actualPower, currentMinPower, maxPower, models)` — `actualPower` comes from routing evidence's `actual_power` field |
| AC2 | On retryable outcomes, `NextMinPower = TopNPowerThreshold(models, 1)` which is > currentMinPower; `MaxPower` is never written to `RetryDecision` |
| AC3 | `computeNextMinPower` calls `TopNPowerThreshold(models, 1)` → top-tier-only threshold |
| AC4 | `RetryDecision` struct has only `ShouldRetry`, `NextMinPower`, `Reason`, `Evidence` — no harness/provider/model/model-ref fields |
| AC5 | `ClassifyRetryOutcome` reads only `failureMode` typed constants; `EvaluateRetryPolicy` takes only power integers — no passthrough string inspection |
| AC6 | `TestEvaluateRetryPolicy_SuccessNoRetry`, `_NoChangesEscalates`, `_ReviewBlockedCapabilityEscalates`, `_DecisionContainsNoPins`, `_MaxPowerPreserved`/`_AlreadyAtTopTier`/`_NoCatalogNoProgress`, `_SetupFailureDoesNotRetry` |
| AC7 | `TestEvaluateRetryPolicy_PassthroughExhaustedConstraint` and `_PassthroughExhaustedPower` — both stop with `ShouldRetry=false`, `NextMinPower=0`, no `ResolveRoute` call in the policy |
| AC8 | `TestEvaluateRetryPolicy_AuditFieldsAlwaysPopulated` — verifies `Reason` and `Evidence` are non-empty across all 8 outcome paths |
